title: Executive Order 9539—Reinstating Avra M. Warren in the Foreign Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An individualized reinstatement order, effective only upon Senate confirmation of the nomination — the same strong disclosed check as EO 9529 (prior batch).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: reinstatement is expressly contingent on Senate confirmation, not the President's own determination alone.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Senate-confirmation contingency is a genuine, disclosed check, not one removed.

title: Executive Order 9538—Authorizing Certification for Probational Appointment of Persons Who Lost Opportunity for Probational Appointment Because of Entry Into the Armed Forces

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, defined-class remedy restoring a specific probational-appointment opportunity lost due to military service, with multiple objective eligibility criteria and an express carve-out preserving pre-existing draft-status objections; benefits-restoring rather than power-concentrating.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective, multi-part eligibility test (dates, discharge status, relative eligibles-list standing, timely request).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9541—Transferring the Office of Surplus Property of the Procurement Division of the Department of the Treasury to the Department of Commerce

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A statutorily-anchored jurisdictional transfer (implementing a Surplus Property Board designation under the Surplus Property Act of 1944) between two Cabinet departments; the joint Secretary-of-Treasury/Secretary-of-Commerce determination requirement for personnel/property transfer is itself a disclosed, non-unilateral mechanism.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed joint-determination requirement between two Cabinet officers, not unilateral fiat.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; implements the Surplus Property Board's own prior regulatory designation.

title: Executive Order 9542—Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Possession of And to Operate the Plants and Facilities of the United Engineering Company, Ltd. Located At San Francisco, California

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template, open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Secretary of the Navy, with Sec. 1 additionally allowing possession of only 'such part or parts as he deems necessary or desirable' — a self-scoped subset determination.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 7, restoration/termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single named company, single site; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To take any other steps that he deems necessary to carry out the provisions and purposes of this order' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 7: termination 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure enforces compliance with 'directive orders of the National War Labor Board dated March 23, 1945, and March 28, 1945' issued against Lodge No. 68, International Association of Machinists — the seizure enforces an already-issued labor ruling with no further review channel.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single company; modest downstream exposure.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 6 directs an unusually broad, explicit cooperation list — 'the Department of Justice, the War Manpower Commission, the National Labor Relations Board, the Department of Labor, the National Selective Service System, and the Office of Price Administration' — and Sec. 5 adds a distinctive new feature not seen in earlier seizure orders this stream: 'upon request of the Secretary of the Navy... the Department of Justice, through the Federal Bureau of Investigation, shall immediately undertake and conduct an investigation of any matter affecting the operation of said plants... or government possession thereof' — an explicit, on-request FBI investigative-support clause layered onto the seizure apparatus.

title: Executive Order 9543—Designating the Reconstruction Finance Corporation as the Agency to Act With Respect to Certain Bonds, Notes, and Other Securities Acquired on Behalf of the United States Under the Provisions of the Transportation Act, 1920

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-anchored designation of RFC as disposal agent for specific railway-loan securities (Seaboard Air Line/Seaboard-Bay Line); bounded financial-agency administration, not a discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed statutory basis; proceeds are required to be 'paid to the Secretary of the Treasury for deposit to the appropriate account' — a genuine, disclosed fiscal-accountability channel.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the Treasury-deposit requirement is itself a disclosed check.

title: Executive Order 9544—Authorizing and Directing the Secretary of War to Assume Full Control of a Certain Airport

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, single-facility (1,520-acre Redmond, Oregon airport) control assumption under a specific 1928 statute; bounded to one named, surveyed property.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single named facility and surveyed legal description; no broader class of properties is implicated.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: The order 'shall continue in force and effect during the unlimited national emergency declared by Proclamation No. 2487 of May 27, 1941... and for six months after the official termination thereof' — by April 1945 this proclamation was already nearly four years old with no independent review date, the same zombie-emergency structure seen in EO 9441/EO 9456 (prior batch).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed date; termination is contingent on the informal, undefined formal termination of the 1941 proclamation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No additional oversight beyond the underlying statute's own terms is removed.

title: Executive Order 9545—Restoring Certain Lands Comprising Part of the Aiea Military Reservation to the Use of the Territory of Hawaii

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, surveyed land-restoration to Territorial use (0.144 acre), with the United States retaining a disclosed reserved right-of-way; narrows rather than expands federal military control.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed metes-and-bounds and reserved right-of-way.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order restores rather than removes territorial jurisdiction.

title: Executive Order 9546—Inspection By the Federal Security Agency of Statistical Transcript Punch Cards Prepared from Individual Income Tax Returns

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants the Federal Security Agency inspection access to anonymized statistical punch cards derived from tax returns; oversight/statistical-analysis-enhancing, not power-concentrating.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: inspection is 'in accordance and upon compliance with the rules and regulations prescribed by the Secretary of the Treasury... approved by me this date.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order expands rather than removes oversight capacity.

title: Executive Order 9547—Providing for Representation of the United States in Preparing and Prosecuting Charges of Atrocities and War Crimes Against the Leaders of the European Axis Powers and Their Principal Agents and Accessories

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Designates Associate Justice Robert H. Jackson as U.S. Representative/Chief of Counsel for an international war-crimes prosecution, serving 'without additional compensation'; the order is fundamentally a personnel-designation and inter-agency-cooperation-authorization instrument, not a domestic power-concentration grant — the agreement to prosecute before an international tribunal is itself contingent on the United States' agreement 'with any of the United Nations,' a multilateral rather than unilateral framework.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope; agency assistance is 'within the limits of appropriations now or hereafter available,' a genuine fiscal boundary.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically foundational despite its narrow textual grant: this order designates the U.S. representative who would go on to lead the prosecution at the Nuremberg trials, establishing legal precedents (the 'Nuremberg principles') that remain foundational to modern international criminal law — a clear case, paralleling EO 9417 (War Refugee Board, prior batch), where an order's historical/legal significance substantially exceeds what its narrow structural-governance text alone would suggest.

title: Executive Order 9548—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to Take Possession of and to Operate Certain Coal Mines

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The second 'standing' nationwide seizure authorization in the coal-seizure family (following EO 9536, prior batch, for bituminous mines): the Secretary of the Interior is authorized to seize 'any and all such mines' producing ANTHRACITE nationwide experiencing 'interruptions or threatened interruptions... as a result of existing labor disturbances,' with no enumerated target list at all — the same unbounded, prospective, self-executing design as EO 9536, now extended to the second major domestic coal type.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The same partial, wage-specific disclosed check as EO 9536 is present: Sec. 3 subjects wage-and-hour changes to 'national wage and price stabilization policies as determined by the National War Labor Board and the Economic Stabilization Director.' But the underlying seizure/operate determination itself remains entirely self-graded by the Secretary, with no external board reviewing which mines are actually seized under this open-ended authorization.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Textually unbounded target class ('any and all such mines' producing anthracite nationwide) — the same maximal bundling design as EO 9536.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such a manner as he may deem necessary for the successful prosecution of the war' is open-ended, applied to an unbounded target class.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 7's termination trigger — possession ends 'as soon as he determines... no longer required... but in no event more than sixty days after' restoration — remains self-graded, and the standing/anticipatory design again allows repeated seize-and-release cycles under a single order without further presidential re-authorization.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed date; the standing/anticipatory design compounds the usual self-graded-termination sunset weakness.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No creditor/judicial-remedy bar comparable to EO 9412/EO 9462/EO 9554 is present in this order's text.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Confirms that EO 9536's standing-authorization design was not a one-off: within three weeks, the same open-ended architecture was replicated for a second major coal category, establishing it as a durable pattern in the seizure-power toolkit rather than an isolated escalation.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 5: 'upon the request of the Secretary of the Interior, the Secretary of War shall take such action... as he may deem necessary or desirable to provide protection' — the same open-ended cross-agency assistance clause as EO 9536/EO 9438/EO 9462.

title: Executive Order 9549—Designation of Mrs. Ruth W. Talley and Mrs. Alice M. Grove to Sign Land Patents

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, individualized signature-delegation for two named General Land Office employees (principal/alternate); purely ministerial.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, named individuals with a defined principal/alternate structure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9550—Relating to Aerial Flights By Personnel of the Coast and Geodetic Survey

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends an already-existing flight-pay regulatory framework (EO 9195, outside this stream's coding window) to Coast and Geodetic Survey personnel, mirroring the Director-of-the-Survey-substituted-for-Chief-of-Naval-Personnel template already established for that agency; no new discretionary power beyond the pre-existing framework's own terms.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed reporting-chain substitution.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9551—Providing for the Transfer to the Republic of Panama of the Water and Sewage Systems Installed By the United States in the Cities of Panama and Colon

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a specific 1943 congressional joint resolution and a bilateral 1942 agreement transferring U.S.-built water/sewage infrastructure to the Republic of Panama; devolves rather than concentrates U.S. authority, and the transfer terms themselves must be 'agreed upon between the authorized representatives' of both governments — a genuine bilateral, not unilateral, mechanism.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed statutory and treaty basis; rate terms are fixed by statute (B/0.09 per 1,000 gallons) pending an independent Panamanian water system.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No U.S. oversight is removed; this order relinquishes U.S. control by agreement.

title: Executive Order 9552—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the Cocker Machine and Foundry Company, Located At Gastonia, North Carolina

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template, open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Secretary of War.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 4, restoration/termination — nominal check only, though Sec. 2's presidential-report-if-insufficient safeguard (below) is a genuine partial exception.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single company, single locality; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any other thing that he may deem necessary for, or incidental to, the operation' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 4: termination 'within 60 days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure enforces compliance with 'the directive orders dated November 1 and 2, 1944, of the Fourth (Atlanta) Regional War Labor Board,' with no further review channel for the underlying dispute.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Sec. 2: accrued WLB-directive wage increases may be paid 'only out of the net operating income of said plants and facilities,' with the same disclosed presidential-report safeguard as EO 9480 (prior batch) if income proves insufficient.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single company; modest downstream exposure.

title: Executive Order 9553—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the River Terminal Railway Company and Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A Railway Labor Act §10 emergency mediation board — a genuinely balanced, disclosed dispute-resolution mechanism: three independent members (statutorily barred from having any pecuniary interest in either carrier or labor organization), a fixed 30-day reporting deadline, and a mutual status-quo requirement binding BOTH the carrier and its employees pending the board's report — structurally the cleanest order-type encountered in this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement ('no member... pecuniarily or otherwise interested') and fixed statutory deadline, not nominal safeguards.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties to the dispute.

title: Executive Order 9554—Possession and Operation of Property of Motor Carriers in and About the City of Chicago, Illinois

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of the Office of Defense Transportation is granted open-ended seizure/operation authority — 'do anything that he may deem necessary to carry out the provisions and purposes of this order' — over motor-carrier plants, facilities, and transportation systems 'in or about the City of Chicago' that 'have been or are about to be interrupted' by labor disturbance, an anticipatory trigger not limited to a pre-enumerated carrier list.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; the Director alone certifies the triggering threat and, per Sec. 4, alone determines termination when possession/control/operation are 'no longer necessary' — a nominal, self-graded check.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Covers an undefined, anticipatory class of Chicago-area motor carriers rather than a single named company — narrower in geographic reach than EO 9462's multi-state 103-carrier list, but structurally the same open-target-class design applied to a metropolitan area.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'Do anything that he may deem necessary' is wholly open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 4's termination trigger is entirely self-judged with no fixed date and, notably, no 60-day outer cap at all — the same self-perpetuation risk pattern as EO 9536/EO 9548/EO 9493.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed sunset; termination is wholly contingent on the Director's own, unreviewed determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 3 explicitly bars, 'except with the prior written consent of the Director,' any 'attachment by mesne process, garnishment, execution, or otherwise' against property in the Director's possession — the same direct creditor/judicial-remedy preemption clause as EO 9412 and EO 9462.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Sec. 2: WLB-directive wage increases which accrued prior to possession may be paid by the Director 'only out of the net operating revenue of the said plant, facility, or transportation system' — the same third-party financial-risk-shifting structure as EO 9462.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A metropolitan-scale motor-freight seizure carries meaningful regional supply-chain exposure, though narrower than EO 9462's multi-state scope; notably, Sec. 7 expressly carves OUT any carrier already under EO 9462 possession, a genuine, disclosed anti-double-seizure safeguard.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 6: 'The Director... may request the Secretary of War to furnish protection... equipment, manpower, and other facilities or services... and the Secretary of War is authorized and directed upon such request to take such action' — the same cross-agency assistance clause family as EO 9462/9536/9548.

title: Executive Order 9555—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Illinois Central Railroad Company and Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template as EO 9553: three independent members, fixed 30-day deadline, mutual status-quo requirement — here covering a dispute affecting eleven states.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9557—Amendment of Executive Order 9210 of August 1, 1942, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Payment of Additional Compensation to Enlisted Men of the Navy and Coast Guard Specially Qualified in the Use of Arms

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, fixed-rate marksmanship-pay schedule amendment; purely arithmetic/administrative.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed five-tier dollar schedule.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9556—Providing for the Exercise of Certain Functions, Duties, and Powers of the President With Respect to Certain Court Martial Cases By the Secretary of War, the Under Secretary of War, the Secretary of the Navy and the Under Secretary of the Navy

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Delegates the President's own Articles of War/Articles for the Government of the Navy court-martial confirmation, commutation, and related clemency-review functions to four named officials, exercisable 'without the necessity of any signature, approval, ratification or other act by the President' — a real, substantive delegation of a significant presidential judicial-review function, though tempered by a genuine, disclosed carve-out described under Flag 2.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed and substantively important safeguard: the delegation explicitly EXCLUDES 'those [sentences] extending to death' (Army) and 'the loss of life' (Navy) — the single most severe category of court-martial outcome is expressly retained for direct presidential review, not delegated. Sec. 4 also expressly preserves the President's own underlying power to act personally on any case notwithstanding the delegation.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The delegated procedure borrows the pre-existing Articles of War/Articles for the Government of the Navy framework wholesale rather than establishing a new, vague standard; delegation is described with specificity (named Articles, named officials).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed statutory sunset: the order 'shall continue in force and effect until the termination of Title I of the First War Powers Act, 1941' — an externally-anchored statutory termination point rather than an indefinitely-renewable presidential proclamation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The capital-case carve-out and the President's own reserved authority to act personally (Sec. 4) are genuine, disclosed limits on the delegation, not oversight removed.

title: Executive Order 9558—Reinstating Certain Former Foreign Service Officers

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Official text was not published; based on the disclosed title, this is an individualized personnel-reinstatement action of the same narrow, non-discretionary type as the routine Foreign Service personnel orders (EO 9539, EO 9529, EO 9457) coded elsewhere in this stream, and is coded conservatively on that basis.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Individualized personnel action; nothing in the disclosed title suggests any discretionary or structural grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is implicated by a personnel reinstatement.

title: Executive Order 9559—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the Gaffney Manufacturing Company, Located At Gaffney, South Carolina

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template, open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Secretary of War.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 4, restoration/termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single company, single locality; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any other thing that he may deem necessary for, or incidental to, the operation' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 4: termination 'within 60 days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure enforces compliance with 'the directive order, dated November 15, 1944, of the Fourth (Atlanta) Regional War Labor Board,' with no further review channel for the underlying dispute.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single company; modest downstream exposure.

title: Executive Order 9560—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the Mary-Leila Cotton Mills, Inc., Located At Greensboro, Georgia

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template, open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Secretary of War.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 4, restoration/termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single company, single locality; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any other thing that he may deem necessary for, or incidental to, the operation' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 4: termination 'within 60 days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure enforces compliance with 'the directive order, dated October 31, 1944, of the Fourth (Atlanta) Regional War Labor Board,' with no further review channel for the underlying dispute.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single company; modest downstream exposure.

title: Executive Order 9561—Amending Executive Order No. 9386 of October 15, 1943, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Granting of Allowances for Quarters and Subsistence to Enlisted Men

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, fixed-rate subsistence-allowance increase ($1.80 to $2.25/day); purely arithmetic/administrative.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed dollar figure.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed FIXED-DATE sunset: 'this order shall be effective from June 1, 1945, until June 30, 1946, unless sooner modified or revoked' — an unusually clean, date-certain sunset, one of the strongest disclosed sunset structures in this batch.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the fixed sunset is itself a disclosed safeguard.

title: Executive Order 9563—Amendment of Rule 46 of Executive Order No. 4314 of September 25, 1925, Establishing Rules Governing Navigation of the Panama Canal and Adjacent Waters

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical meal-furnishing obligation rule for Canal transit crews; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed obligation with a genuine disclosed cost-shifting fallback (Canal furnishes meals 'at the expense of the vessel' if the vessel cannot).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9562—Termination of the Office of Civilian Defense

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of the Bureau of the Budget is granted broad wind-down discretion — 'such further measures and dispositions as may be determined by the Director... shall be carried out in such manner as the Director may direct and by such agencies as he may designate' — an open-ended residual-authority clause governing an entire agency's dissolution, paralleling the earlier EO 9471 (OEM Central Administrative Services termination, prior batch) template.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Substantially bounded by disclosed, specific statutory anchors: property disposition follows 'the Surplus Property Act of 1944 and the regulations and designations of the Surplus Property Board,' and Treasury's wind-up role (personnel separation, obligation liquidation, records disposition) is itemized in detail — a real, disclosed procedural structure constraining most of the wind-down, even though the residual clause (Flag 1) remains open.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: The residual 'as may be determined... to be necessary' clause is open-ended.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Surplus Property Board's regulatory framework is expressly preserved, not displaced.

title: Executive Order 9564—Authorizing the Petroleum Administrator to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the Humble Oil and Refining Company Located in Ingleside, Texas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template, open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Petroleum Administrator, with Sec. 6's further delegation to the Deputy Petroleum Administrator — an internal sub-delegation layer distinctive to the Petroleum Administration seizure family.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Administrator self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 5, restoration/termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single company, single site; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any other thing that he may deem necessary for, or incidental to, the operation' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 5: termination 'within 60 days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure enforces compliance with 'the directive order dated April 1, 1944, of the National War Labor Board,' with no further review channel for the underlying dispute.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single refining facility; modest downstream exposure.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 4 directs 'all Federal agencies, including, but not limited to, the War Manpower Commission, the National Selective Service System, and the Department of Justice' to cooperate, and Sec. 3 authorizes the Secretary of War to furnish protection upon the Administrator's request — an open-ended cross-agency assistance clause.

title: Executive Order 9565—Authorizing the Petroleum Administrator to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities At the Cabin Creek Oil Field of the Pure Oil Company Located At Dawes, West Virginia

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template, open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Petroleum Administrator, with the same Deputy Petroleum Administrator sub-delegation layer as EO 9564.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Administrator self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 5, restoration/termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single oil field, single company; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any other thing that he may deem necessary for, or incidental to, the operation' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 5: termination 'within 60 days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Seizure enforces compliance with 'the directive order dated August 25, 1944, of the Fifth Regional War Labor Board,' with no further review channel for the underlying dispute.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single oil field; modest downstream exposure.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: The same 'War Manpower Commission, the National Selective Service System, and the Department of Justice' cooperation clause and Secretary-of-War protection-request mechanism as EO 9564.

title: Executive Order 9566—Revocation of Executive Order 9090 of March 6, 1942, Establishing An Airspace Reservation Over Portions of Ulster and Dutchess Counties, New York

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A pure revocation of a prior airspace-reservation order; contracts rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single named prior order revoked.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9567—Amending Executive Order No. 9095, as Amended by Executive Order No. 9193, to Define Further the Functions and Duties of the Alien Property Custodian With Respect to Property of Germany and Japan and Nationals Thereof

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A diff-aware, NARROWING amendment to the already-CRITICAL EO 9095/9193 Alien Property Custodian definitions (both outside this batch's coding window): the amended Sec. 2(c) carves liquid financial assets (cash, bullion, currencies, credits, securities) OUT of automatic vesting scope for enemy countries/nationals OTHER than Germany and Japan, while leaving the full, broader definition intact for Germany/Japan specifically — a protective narrowing of reach for minor Axis-satellite nationals rather than a further concentration of the Custodian's vesting power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The narrowing is itself the disclosed protective feature; scope for non-Germany/Japan nationals is genuinely reduced, not merely re-labeled.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored in EO 9095/9193; if anything, discretion is narrowed for most affected nationals.

flags.name: Exemptions Architecture
flags.justification: The amended clause carves an explicit exemption from vesting for most liquid assets of non-Germany/Japan enemy nationals, 'except to the extent that the Alien Property Custodian determines that such... are necessary for the maintenance or safeguarding of other property... subject to vesting' — a disclosed exemption whose boundary is set by the Custodian's own discretionary determination, a nominal rather than fully external check on the exemption's scope.

title: Executive Order 9568—Providing for the Release of Scientific Information

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of War Mobilization and Reconversion is authorized to REVIEW and RECOMMEND declassification of wartime scientific information, but final decision-making authority over whether 'national military security permits' release rests explicitly with the originating classifying department (Secretary of War or Navy) under Sec. 3(b) — the Director's role is advisory/recommendatory, not a final unilateral grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed multi-layer check: an interdepartmental Publication Board (Attorney General, Secretaries of Interior/Agriculture/Commerce/Labor, plus War/Navy/OWI/OSRD/NACA liaison officers) advises the Director, AND the classifying department's Secretary retains final say per Sec. 3(b) — a real disclosed structure, not merely nominal.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent purpose (scientific-information declassification review).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In so far as practicable' and procedures 'as may be adopted by him' leave meaningful operational discretion to the Director, though bounded by the final-say structure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The classifying department's retained final authority (Sec. 3(b)) and the President's own reserved 'closed field' exclusion power (Sec. 2) are genuine, disclosed limits on the Director's role, not oversight removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as an early institutional mechanism for post-war declassification of wartime scientific research (an antecedent of the broader postwar science-policy openness debates that would shape U.S. research culture); flagged for its second-order significance despite the modest textual grant.

title: Executive Order 9569—Inspection By the Office of Price Administration of Corporation Statistical Transcript Cards Prepared from Income and Declared Value Excess Profits Tax Returns

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants OPA inspection access to anonymized statistical transcript cards derived from corporate tax returns for a bounded historical window; oversight/analysis-enhancing, not power-concentrating.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: inspection is conditioned on Treasury-prescribed rules and their amendments.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order expands rather than removes oversight capacity.

title: Executive Order 9571—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to Acquire and Dispose of Certain Property

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, surveyed-tract (92.64-acre Cerritos Channel) acquisition/disposal authorization under Title II of the Second War Powers Act, with an express protective purpose (preventing oil/gas drainage loss to the United States) and a genuine fiscal-accountability requirement (royalties 'paid into the Treasury... credited to miscellaneous receipts').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, surveyed description and disclosed fiscal channel.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9572—Possession, Control, and Operation of the Toledo, Peoria & Western Railroad

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A ratification-and-continuation order responding directly to a federal district court opinion that had cast doubt on ODT's continued possession authority following the later-superseded EO 9412 (outside this batch's coding window); this order neither creates new authority nor expands the scope of the ALREADY-established EO 9108/9320 possession (both outside this batch's coding window) — it confirms and continues an existing, previously-scored arrangement in direct response to judicial scrutiny.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Unlike nearly every other seizure-family order in this stream, this order exists BECAUSE a federal court (District Court for the Southern District of Illinois) actively engaged with and questioned the legal basis for continued possession in a suit brought by the railroad itself — a genuine, disclosed instance of judicial review functioning as intended, which this order responds to and resolves rather than forecloses.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order is a direct response to, not a preemption of, judicial scrutiny — the opposite of the Flag 7 concern this rubric tracks.

title: Executive Order 9573—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Georgia and Florida Railroad and Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template as EO 9553/9555: three independent members, fixed 30-day deadline, mutual status-quo requirement — here covering a tri-state dispute.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9574—Authorizing the Secretary of War to Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of the Diamond Alkali Company, Located in Or Around Painesville, Ohio

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template, open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Secretary of War.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 5, restoration/termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single company, single locality; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any other thing that he may deem necessary for, or incidental to, the operation' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 5: termination 'within 60 days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 2 subjects operation to Section 5 of the War Labor Disputes Act generally; unlike several peer orders this batch, no specific already-issued WLB directive is cited as the enforcement basis.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single company; modest downstream exposure.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 4 directs 'all Federal agencies, including but not limited to the War Manpower Commission, the National Selective Service System, and the Department of Justice' to cooperate — open-ended, unenumerated.

title: Executive Order 9575—Declaring the Commissioned Corps of the Public Health Service to Be a Military Service and Prescribing Regulations Therefor

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A presidential declaration alone brings an entire civilian-adjacent professional corps under military criminal jurisdiction (the Articles for the Government of the Navy, including courts-martial with imprisonment/hard-labor punishment) — a significant jurisdictional extension accomplished unilaterally, without a fresh act of Congress specific to this application.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Substantial, genuine disclosed safeguards temper the grant: the authority to CONVENE a general court-martial or court of inquiry 'may not be delegated to any other officer of the Public Health Service' (a real non-delegation rule), and the courts-martial themselves 'have the same powers and authority as other general courts-martial... under the Articles for the Government of the Navy' — i.e., PHS officers are folded into the SAME standing, well-established Navy military-justice procedural framework other servicemembers already use, not a bespoke or looser tribunal.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single coherent purpose (military-justice-jurisdiction extension).

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Procedures are borrowed wholesale from the pre-existing, well-established Naval Courts and Boards, 1937 framework rather than left to open definition.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No existing oversight is removed; the order incorporates an existing, established judicial framework rather than displacing one.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A significant historical second-order effect worth flagging: this order extends military criminal jurisdiction — including corporal/imprisonment punishment — to the Public Health Service Commissioned Corps, a consequential and lasting change to that corps' legal status that persists (in modified form) into its modern role as one of the eight uniformed services.

title: Executive Order 9576—Creating an Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Erie Railroad Company and Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template as EO 9553/9555/9573: three independent members, fixed 30-day deadline, mutual status-quo requirement — here covering a six-state dispute.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9578—Approving Regulations of the Civil Service Commission Under the Federal Employees Pay Act of 1945

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extraordinarily long and detailed set of CSC-promulgated overtime-pay, within-grade-advancement, and night-differential regulations implementing a specific 1945 statute; issued by the multi-member, independent Civil Service Commission (not a single line executive) with exhaustive, objective numeric formulas and defined exception classes.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: About as far from an unchecked, vague grant as an order in this stream gets: every computation is a fixed formula or schedule, every exception class is enumerated, and the Commission (three named Commissioners) itself is the promulgating body.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Length and detail notwithstanding, this is a single coherent statutory-implementation exercise (Federal Employees Pay Act administration), not a bundling of distinct, unrelated grants.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; implements Congress's own pay statute in exhaustive, objective detail.

title: Executive Order 9579—Amendment of Executive Order No. 8937 of November 7, 1941, Extending the Period of Elibibility On Civil Service Registers Or Lists of Persons Who Serve in the Armed Forces of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, technical amendment adding a defined 90-day post-service notification requirement to an existing veterans'-eligibility-extension order; benefits-clarifying, not power-concentrating.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed 90-day window, with retroactive effective date disclosed (December 8, 1944).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9580—Amendment of Executive Order No. 1888 of February 2, 1914, as Amended, Relating to Conditions of Employment in the Service of the Panama Canal and the Panama Railroad Company On the Isthmus of Panama

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, protective leave-pay computation amendment (ensuring leave/travel-leave pay matches what an employee would have earned on duty) for Panama Canal/Railroad employees; purely administrative and worker-protective, with an express anti-retroactive-reduction safeguard (Sec. 4).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed computation rule and a genuine, disclosed non-retroactivity safeguard.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the non-retroactivity clause is itself a disclosed protective safeguard.

title: Executive Order 9581—Amending Executive Order No. 9299 of February 4, 1943, to Give the Territorial War Labor Board for the Territory of Hawaii Jurisdiction Over Voluntary Wage and Salary Adjustments of Employees in the Territory of Hawaii Subject to the Railway Labor Act

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow jurisdictional grant to an existing territorial board over a defined class of voluntary wage adjustments; no new discretionary power beyond the pre-existing EO 9299 framework (outside this batch's coding window).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, bounded jurisdictional scope.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9582—Restoring Land to the Territory of Hawaii for Aeronautical Purposes, and Reserving Land for Military Purposes

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, surveyed dual land-adjustment (1.67 acres restored to Territory for aeronautical use, 1.67 acres reserved for military use), with a genuine disclosed reservation preserving military aircraft landing/takeoff access on the restored parcel; bounded, not open-ended.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed metes-and-bounds and reserved-use provisos.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9583—Amendment of Executive Order 9356 of June 24, 1943, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Furnishing of Clothing in Kind Or Payment of Cash Allowances in Lieu Thereof to Enlisted Personnel of the Navy, the Coast Guard, the Naval Reserve, and the Coast Guard Reserve

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, fixed-rate clothing-allowance schedule update for fiscal year 1946; purely arithmetic/administrative.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed dollar figures.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9584—Suspending Certain Statutory Provisions Relating to Employment in the Canal Zone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow statutory-provision suspension tied to three specific 1946 appropriation acts governing Canal Zone employment; not a general power concentration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Disclosed statutory basis; suspension scope is defined by the underlying appropriation acts' own provisions.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Suspension runs 'during the continuance of any of the wars in which the United States is now engaged' — an open-ended wartime duration with no fixed date and no independent review trigger, the identical zombie-emergency structure as EO 9456/EO 9441 (prior batch) — notably still being renewed annually even as the shooting war was ending in the same month this order issued.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed expiration; termination is contingent on the informal, undefined end of 'any of the wars... now engaged.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No additional oversight beyond the underlying appropriation acts' own terms is removed.

title: Executive Order 9585—Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy To Take Possession of and Operate the Plants and Facilities of Goodyear Tire & Rubber Company Inc., Located at Akron, Ohio

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Standard single-company seizure template, open-ended possession/operation/contractual-rights authority vested in the Secretary of the Navy, with Sec. 1 allowing possession of only 'such part or parts as he deems necessary or desirable' — the same self-scoped-subset feature as EO 9542.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; Secretary self-certifies trigger and, per Sec. 7, restoration/termination — nominal check only.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single named company, single site; standard single-seizure scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'To do any and all other things that he may deem necessary or desirable' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 7: termination 'within sixty days after he determines' restoration — self-graded, non-fixed-date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 3 subjects operation to Section 5 of the War Labor Disputes Act generally; unlike EO 9542 issued three months earlier for the same company family of orders, no specific already-issued WLB directive is cited as the trigger here.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single (if very large) company; the seizure itself is bounded to one Akron facility complex.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: The same distinctive FBI-investigation-on-request clause as EO 9542 (Sec. 5: 'Upon the request of the Secretary of the Navy... the Department of Justice, through the Federal Bureau of Investigation, shall immediately undertake and conduct an investigation') plus the same broad six-agency cooperation list (Sec. 6), confirming this as an established template feature rather than a one-off.

title: Executive Order 9586—The Medal of Freedom

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes an honorary civilian decoration for wartime meritorious service (predecessor to the modern Presidential Medal of Freedom), administered by three Cabinet Secretaries jointly (State, War, Navy) rather than a single official, and expressly excluded from award to U.S. citizens for domestic acts or to servicemembers — a bounded, disclosed scope.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, tri-Secretary joint design-approval and award authority, with regulations required to be 'of uniform application' insofar as practicable.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9588—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate the Disputes Between the Chicago, North Shore, & Milwaukee Railroad Company and the Chicago Aurora & Elgin Railroad Company and Their Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template as EO 9553/9555/9573/9576 (this and prior batch): three independent members, fixed 30-day deadline, mutual status-quo requirement — here covering two carriers jointly.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9589-A—Authorizing the Petroleum Administrator To Take Possession of and Operate the Butadiene Plant Operated by Sinclair Rubber, Inc., at Houston, Texas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: CORPUS/DB NUMBERING MISMATCH DISCLOSED: the DB record for EO 9589 carries the title 'Executive Order 9589-A—...Sinclair Rubber, Inc., at Houston, Texas' (July 19, 1945), but the locally-scraped corpus file at EO-9589.json contains an unrelated order, 'Executive Order 9589—Restricting Competition in Certain Civil Service Examinations to Veterans' (July 16, 1945) — the same '-A' suffix/base-number divergence pattern as EO 9242/9242-A, EO 9395/9395-B, EO 9461/9461-A, and EO 9475/9475-A. CONFIRMED by cross-reference: EO 9603 (Aug. 25, 1945, this batch, 'Termination of Possession of Certain Property Taken By the Government') explicitly lists 'No. 9589A of July 19, 1945' among the seizure orders being wound down, corroborating both the existence and the July 19, 1945 date of a genuine Sinclair Rubber Butadiene Plant seizure order. Coded via title-pattern-matching against the standard Petroleum Administrator single-facility seizure template established by EO 9564/EO 9565 (this batch).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Standard nominal self-certified check, per the Petroleum Administrator seizure template.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Single named plant; standard single-seizure scope, per the template.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Standard open-ended 'as he deems necessary' operational discretion typical of this order class.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: No distinct perverse-incentive structure beyond Flag 6's self-certified termination, per the template.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Standard self-graded, non-fixed-date termination, per the template.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No specific WLB-directive citation is confirmed by the title alone; coded conservatively as ABSENT absent full text.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Single facility; modest downstream exposure, consistent with peer single-facility seizures.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: The Petroleum Administrator seizure template in this batch (EO 9564, EO 9565) consistently includes a broad multi-agency cooperation clause and Secretary-of-War protection-request mechanism; applied here by pattern-matching given the confirmed order family.

title: Executive Order 9591—Amending the Foreign Service Regulations of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow fee-tariff and terminology cleanup amendment (revoking one obsolete fee item, renaming 'disbursing officers' to 'disbursing agents'); purely administrative.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, itemized revisions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9590—Establishing Certain Awards for the Coast and Geodetic Survey

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes a defined set of honorary service ribbons/medals for Coast and Geodetic Survey personnel, administered by the Secretary of Commerce under prescribed regulations; a symbolic recognition program, not a coercive power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed award criteria and a genuine anti-duplication safeguard (War Zone ribbons not awarded to those eligible for equivalent Army/Navy campaign medals).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9592-A—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9400 of December 3, 1943, Authorizing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Possession of and Operate the Shipyard of the Los Angeles Shipbuilding and Drydock Corporation At Los Angeles, California

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: CORPUS/DB NUMBERING MISMATCH DISCLOSED: the DB record for EO 9592 carries the title 'Executive Order 9592-A—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9400... Los Angeles Shipbuilding and Drydock Corporation' (July 23, 1945), but the locally-scraped corpus file at EO-9592.json contains an unrelated order (an EO 9001-extension order to OWI/Alien Property Custodian, July 21, 1945) — the same numbering-mismatch pattern documented repeatedly this stream. EO 9400 itself (Dec. 3, 1943, the underlying LA Shipbuilding seizure) is independently corroborated as a genuine seizure order by its appearance in EO 9603's termination list (this batch) as 'No. 8400 of Dec. 3, 1943' (evident OCR/transcription digit error for 9400). Absent the actual amendment text, this record is coded CONSERVATIVELY as a narrow, diff-aware amendment to an already-established (outside this batch's coding window) seizure order — consistent with the treatment given to other confirmed-but-textually-unavailable diff-aware amendments in this stream (e.g., EO 9429 amending EO 9406) — rather than assumed to expand the underlying grant, since amendments of this type in this corpus have more often narrowed or adjusted procedural terms than expanded seizure scope.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No basis in the (unavailable) text to assume a new or narrowed check; coded conservatively as clean pending any future text recovery.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No basis to assume oversight was removed by a title-only amendment record.

title: Executive Order 9594—Designating the Chief of Engineers, United States Army, as a Member of the Federal Fire Council in Lieu of the Quartermaster General

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Official text was not published; based on the disclosed title, this is a narrow, single-seat membership-substitution on an advisory council, of the same non-discretionary type as other unpublished personnel/designation orders in this stream, and is coded conservatively on that basis.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Individualized council-seat substitution; nothing in the disclosed title suggests any discretionary or structural grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is implicated by a single council-seat substitution.

title: Executive Order 9596—Revoking Executive Order No. 9528 Which Provides a Change in the Order of Succession of Officers to Act as Secretary of the Navy

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A pure revocation of a prior internal succession-order amendment (EO 9528, prior batch), reverting to the earlier succession sequence; contracts rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single named prior order revoked.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9597—Amending Executive Order No. 9240 Entitled "Regulations Relating to Overtime Wage Compensation"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow amendment adding V-J Day to a fixed, enumerated list of premium-pay holidays under an already-existing overtime-compensation order; purely administrative, and issued the very day of the Japanese surrender announcement.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed enumerated holiday list.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9598—Modifying the Conditions Upon Which a Classified Status May Be Granted

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Updates and supersedes EO 9506 (prior batch) with a slightly more detailed grandfathering structure for pre-1942/1943 appointments, while expressly preserving the separate veterans'-restoration mechanism of EO 9538 (this batch); a technical refinement, not an expansion of discretionary power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective multi-part grandfather criteria (appointment date, eligibles-list standing, CSC records).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; if anything, the order clarifies which prior protective order (EO 9538) survives.

title: Executive Order 9599—Providing for Assistance to Expanded Production and Continued Stabilization of the National Economy During the Transition from War to Peace, and for the Orderly Modification of Wartime Controls Over Prices, Wages, Materials and Facilities

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Places the Director of War Mobilization and Reconversion in overall 'guidance and direction' of the entire war-to-peace economic transition, coordinating the War Production Board, Office of Price Administration, National War Labor Board, Secretary of Agriculture, and Federal Loan Administrator simultaneously — the broadest apex-coordinator role encountered in the stream, though notably deployed to manage CONTROLLED DECONTROL rather than to expand substantive controls.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Mixed picture: Title III's price-adjustment authority is expressly made 'subject to such directives... as may be issued by the Economic Stabilization Director,' and Title IV.2 requires Director-of-Economic-Stabilization approval before certain wage increases with price implications take effect — real, disclosed internal checks — but the overall transition is coordinated by, and ultimately answers to, the Director of War Mobilization and Reconversion alone, with no external board reviewing the transition strategy as a whole.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles price-control modification authority (Title III), wage-control modification authority (Title IV), and production-control wind-down authority (Title V) across at least five separate agencies into a single coordinated policy framework — the most extensive cross-agency substantive-policy bundle encountered in this stream, exceeding even EO 9380's institutional consolidation.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As rapidly as feasible without endangering...,' 'as may be necessary,' and similar open standards recur throughout all five Titles.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Economic Stabilization Director's retained approval authority over price-affecting wage increases (Title IV.2) is a genuine, disclosed check, not one removed.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Title IV.1: wage or salary increases negotiated under this order's relaxed approval process are conditioned on a disclosed no-price-pass-through promise — employers/employees may not use the increase 'as the basis for seeking an increase in price ceilings... or for resisting otherwise justifiable reductions in price ceilings' — a real, disclosed constraint shifting risk onto the negotiating parties rather than the price-control system.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: This is the foundational policy framework for the entire 1945-46 postwar reconversion period — the controlled unwinding of price, wage, and production controls that shaped the era's inflation debates and the historic strike wave of late 1945/1946 — arguably one of the most consequential single economic-policy orders in the Truman corpus to date.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Simultaneous coordination of WPB, OPA, NWLB, the Secretary of Agriculture, and the Federal Loan Administrator under a single Director's 'guidance and direction' is itself the apex-coordinator pattern already flagged for EO 9347/EO 9380 (prior batch), now applied economy-wide during the transition.

title: Executive Order 9600—Amending Executive Order No. 9240 Entitled "Regulations Relating to Overtime Wage Compensation"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A further narrow amendment refining the same holiday list (substituting specific V-J Day observance dates, August 15-16, 1945, for the general 'V-J Day' reference in EO 9597 four days earlier); purely administrative and short-lived (superseded by outright revocation three days later, EO 9601).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed enumerated holiday list.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9601—Revocation of Executive Order 9240 as Amended, Entitled "Regulations Relating to Overtime Compensation"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Outright revocation of the wartime overtime-compensation order (and its EO 9597/9600 amendments, this batch) effective immediately upon the war's end; narrows rather than expands authority — the clearest possible example of a wartime control being voluntarily and promptly relinquished.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, unconditional revocation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order removes a wartime control mechanism entirely rather than removing oversight of one.

title: Executive Order 9602—Possession, Control, and Operation of the Transportation System, Plants, and Facilities of the Illinois Central Railroad Company

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of the Office of Defense Transportation is granted open-ended seizure/operation authority — 'in such a manner as he may deem necessary to carry out the provisions, and accomplish the purposes of this order' — over the entire Illinois Central Railroad transportation system.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; the Director alone certifies the triggering threat and, per Sec. 4, alone determines termination — a nominal, self-graded check.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: A single named railroad; narrower in bundling scope than EO 9554's undefined Chicago motor-carrier class or EO 9462's 103-carrier list.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such a manner as he may deem necessary' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 4's termination trigger is entirely self-judged with no fixed date and no 60-day outer cap at all — the same self-perpetuation risk pattern as EO 9536/EO 9548/EO 9554/EO 9493.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed sunset; termination is wholly contingent on the Director's own, unreviewed determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 3 explicitly bars, 'except with the prior written consent of the Director,' any 'attachment by mesne process, garnishment, execution, or otherwise' against property in the Director's possession — the same direct creditor/judicial-remedy preemption clause as EO 9412, EO 9462, and EO 9554, now appearing for the third time in roughly a year across three different seizure targets (rail, trucking, rail again).

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A single (if major) railroad's freight/passenger system; significant but narrower in scope than a multi-carrier or multi-state seizure.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 6: 'The Director... may request the Secretary of War to furnish protection... equipment, manpower, and other facilities or services... and the Secretary of War is authorized and directed upon such request to take such action' — the same cross-agency assistance clause family as EO 9462/9536/9548/9554.

title: Executive Order 9604—Providing for the Release of Scientific Information (Extension and Amendment of Executive Order No. 9568)

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Extends the already-scored EO 9568 (this batch) Publication Board/Director review mechanism to captured ENEMY scientific/industrial information rather than domestic wartime research; the same advisory/recommendatory structure applies, with Sec. 3 expressly reaffirming that 'nothing in this order shall be construed to limit or modify the power of the Secretary of War or the Secretary of the Navy to determine finally whether the national military security permits' release.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The same genuine, disclosed final-say safeguard as EO 9568 is expressly reaffirmed and even emphasized here ('nothing... shall be construed to limit or modify').

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The classifying department's final say is expressly reaffirmed, not removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Extends the declassification-review mechanism to enemy-origin technical/scientific data seized at war's end (e.g., German and Japanese wartime research) — a historically significant channel for postwar technology transfer, paralleling the broader (extra-legal and separately controversial) programs like Operation Paperclip in bringing enemy technical knowledge into public/commercial use; flagged for its second-order historical significance despite the modest, well-checked textual grant.

title: Executive Order 9603—Termination of Possession of Certain Property Taken By the Government

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The master wind-down order for the wartime seizure-power apparatus: rather than concentrating new authority, this order directs the return of ALL property seized under a listed set of ~26 prior seizure orders (including EO 9108, 9225, 9351, 9400, 9462, 9463, 9466, 9475-A, 9484, 9493, 9505, 9508, 9516, 9536, 9540, 9542, 9552, 9559, 9560, 9564, 9565, 9577-A, 9585, 9589-A, 9593, 9595 — spanning coal mines, railroads, motor carriers, and dozens of individual plants across this and the prior batch) 'as soon as practicable' — the definitive structural bookend to the WWII plant/mine/transportation-seizure era in this stream.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Notably ADDS a genuine, disclosed accountability layer absent from the individual seizure orders' own termination clauses: return determinations by 'the officer by whom the property in question is held and operated' now require 'the approval of the Director of Economic Stabilization' — an independent second-signature requirement layered onto what was, in each original order, a purely self-graded determination by the seizing official alone.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Consolidates the wind-down of roughly 26 separately-issued seizure orders into a single order — a large bundling event, though (as with EO 9526, prior batch) in the accountability-restoring rather than power-concentrating direction.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order restores rather than removes private ownership/control across the listed properties.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: The clearest structural bookend in this entire stream to date: this single order marks the mass return of virtually every outstanding wartime seizure captured in this and the prior batch, a defining marker of the transition from a war economy to a peacetime economy and a fitting counterpart to EO 9536/EO 9548/EO 9554/EO 9602's escalating seizure-power architecture just months earlier.

title: Executive Order 9605—Revoking Paragraph 4 of Executive Order No. 9279 of December 5, 1942, So as to Permit Voluntary Enlistments in the Armed Forces

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, liberty-restoring revocation permitting voluntary enlistments again (likely a wartime manpower-allocation restriction being lifted at war's end); narrows rather than expands executive control.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single-paragraph revocation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order removes a restriction rather than removing oversight.

title: Executive Order 9606—Adopting and Ratifying the Capture of the German Motor Vessel "Caribia"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, retroactive ratification of a single specific prize-of-war capture already effected by a named War Shipping Administration officer; no forward-looking discretionary grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single named vessel and named capturing officer.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9609—Authorizing the National Advisory Committee for Aeronautics to Acquire and Dispose of Property

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-anchored (Second War Powers Act Title II) property-acquisition authorization for a single named agency, bounded to property 'deemed necessary for military, naval, or other war purposes'; matches the established, consistently-clean Title II template (EO 9571, this batch; EO 9610 below).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed statutory basis and bounded purpose.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9610—Authorizing the Administrator of Veterans' Affairs to Acquire and Dispose of Property

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same Second War Powers Act Title II property-acquisition template as EO 9609, here for the Veterans' Administration, expressly grounded in a specific statutory 'essential war agency' designation and a disclosed factual finding (acute space shortage).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed statutory basis, designation, and factual finding.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9611—Reestablishing the Advisory Board on Just Compensation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Re-establishes a genuinely independent, three-member judicial advisory board (federal appellate/district judges) to set fair-compensation standards for War Shipping Administration vessel requisitions — the WSA is directed to 'be guided by' the Board's determinations, a real, disclosed subordination of the seizing agency to an independent expert body, not a power concentration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence: the Board consists of named sitting federal judges, and the WSA 'shall be guided by the determination of the Board' — a substantive, not nominal, check.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an independent oversight body.

title: Executive Order 9612—Amending Executive Order 8902 Prescribing Regulations Pertaining to the Entry of Coffee Into the United States from Countries Signatories of the Inter-american Coffee Agreement

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow amendment adding an automatic-suspension trigger tied to the operative status of a specific international agreement provision (Article VI, Inter-American Coffee Agreement); self-limiting rather than power-expanding.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
