flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: the underlying regulation's own force is made contingent on an EXTERNAL, internationally-determined trigger (the Coffee Board's action or the Agreement's expiration), with a disclosed notification chain (State to Treasury).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the external-trigger dependency is itself a disclosed limitation.

title: Executive Order 9613—Withdrawing and Reserving for the Use of the United States Lands Containing Radio-active Mineral Substances

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A broad-in-geography but narrow-in-subject land withdrawal (all U.S. public lands, including Alaska, containing radioactive mineral deposits) under general presidential public-land-withdrawal authority; matches the standard land-reservation template, expressly made 'subject to valid existing rights.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed preserved-rights safeguard ('subject to valid existing rights,' and Sec. 2's proviso that no reservation shall interfere with Congressionally-established land uses).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Existing rights and Congressional land-use designations are expressly preserved, not displaced.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant timing and substance: issued exactly one month after the atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, this is the first executive assertion of blanket federal control over radioactive mineral deposits nationwide — a direct structural precursor to the federal uranium-control regime formalized the following year by the Atomic Energy Act of 1946, worth flagging as an early marker of the dawning atomic age's domestic-resource-control architecture.

title: Executive Order 9614—Termination of the War Refugee Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Terminates the War Refugee Board (EO 9417, prior batch) and directs an orderly, statutorily-bounded wind-down; narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed fixed-date wind-down deadline ('not later than June 30, 1946') and a defined winding-up procedure (personnel separation, property/records disposition) assigned to the Secretary of the Treasury.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the fixed deadline is itself a disclosed accountability mechanism.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Closes the chapter on one of this stream's clearest examples (EO 9417) of an order whose historical/moral significance exceeded its structural score; the termination itself is a clean administrative wind-down and does not independently carry comparable significance, so no flag is warranted here despite the underlying institution's importance.

title: Executive Order 9616—Relating to the United States High Commissioner to the Philippine Islands

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: CORPUS/DB NUMBERING MISMATCH DISCLOSED: the DB record for EO 9616 carries the title 'Relating to the United States High Commissioner to the Philippine Islands,' but the locally-scraped corpus file at EO-9616.json instead contains the text of a differently-numbered order, 'Executive Order 9616-A—Creation of a Special Temporary Position in the Public Health Service' (an unrelated PHS personnel action). Absent the actual text for the DB-titled order, this record is coded CONSERVATIVELY by title-pattern-matching against the many narrow, individualized territorial/personnel-designation orders already coded in this stream relating to a single high-level office (e.g., EO 9618 below, Puerto Rico Reconstruction Administrator), scored as a presumptively narrow administrative/personnel order pending any future text recovery.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No basis in the (unavailable) text to assume a discretionary or structural grant; coded conservatively as clean.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No basis to assume oversight was removed by a title-only record.

title: Executive Order 9615—Making Certain Time Limitations Imposed By Executive Order No. 4601 of March 1, 1927, Prescribing Regulations Pertaining to the Award of the Distinguished Flying Cross, Inapplicable to Certain Cases

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, narrow waiver of an administrative filing deadline for Distinguished Flying Cross recommendations arising from WWII service, bounded by two objective conditions (act performed during the war window; recommendation initiated within six months of hostilities' termination).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective dual-condition test tied to an externally-proclaimed 'date of the termination of hostilities.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; this order relaxes a technical deadline rather than removing a substantive check.

title: Executive Order 9618—Appointment of Edwin G. Arnold, Director, Division of Territories and Island Possessions, Department of the Interior, as Administrator of the Puerto Rico Reconstruction Adminstration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An individualized, unpaid appointment to an already-established office (Puerto Rico Reconstruction Administrator, under 1935-37 orders outside this batch's coding window), exercising only the functions already conferred by those prior orders; no new discretionary power is created.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, named individual, serving 'without additional compensation.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No new discretion beyond what was already scored (or would be scored) in the underlying 1935-37 orders.

title: Executive Order 9617—Transfer of Certain Agencies and Functions to the Department of Labor

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Transfers three major wartime institutions — the War Manpower Commission (terminated outright, Sec. 1(c)), the National War Labor Board, and the Retraining and Reemployment Administration — into the Department of Labor under the Secretary's 'supervision, direction, and control,' a substantial permanent consolidation of previously separate wartime-mobilization machinery into a single Cabinet department.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A genuinely mixed picture: Sec. 2(b) contains an important, disclosed carve-out preserving NWLB adjudicative independence — 'decisions made by the National War Labor Board in individual cases... shall NOT be subject to modification by the Secretary of Labor but shall have the same degree of finality as existed prior to the issuance of this order' — even as the Board's administrative/policy functions ARE subordinated to the Secretary. This genuine partial safeguard is scored as a nominal/nonbinding check overall (PRESENT) because it protects only the adjudicative function, not the Board's broader institutional independence, which is fully absorbed.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles three distinct major wartime agencies (WMC, NWLB, Retraining and Reemployment Administration) into a single consolidating transfer, with a further split-off of WMC's Procurement and Assignment Service to a FOURTH agency (Federal Security Administrator, Sec. 5) — an unusually complex multi-directional bundling event.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: Sec. 4(a): the Secretary 'may perform the functions transferred to him by this order through such officials, persons, or agencies and in such manner as he shall determine' — open-ended.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The NWLB individual-case finality preservation (Sec. 2(b)) is a genuine, disclosed check specifically protecting adjudicative outcomes from being reopened, not one removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: One of the major institutional-consolidation events of the immediate postwar period: the permanent absorption of the WWII labor-mobilization apparatus (manpower allocation, wage-dispute adjudication, veteran retraining) into the Department of Labor reshaped that department's structure and mission on a lasting basis, well beyond the wartime emergency that originally justified these agencies' creation.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: The simultaneous four-way split (WMC/NWLB/RRA to Labor, Procurement and Assignment Service to the Federal Security Administrator) creates a genuinely fragmented, multi-agency transfer requiring ongoing inter-agency coordination between Labor and the Federal Security Agency for functions (employment services, unemployment compensation data) that had previously been unified under one wartime commission.

title: Executive Order 9619—Amending Subdivision Vii of Schedule a of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Adds a single named exempted position (a private secretary) to the Civil Service Rules' Schedule A exemption list; a trivial, individualized administrative addition.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single named position.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9620—Abolishing the Office of Economic Stabilization and Transferring Its Functions to the Office of War Mobilization and Reconversion

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Transfers the Office of Economic Stabilization's functions to the ALREADY-established, already-accountable Director of War Mobilization and Reconversion (whose own authority derives from the War Mobilization and Reconversion Act of 1944, outside this batch's coding window); consistent with the 'reorganization to accountable official reads clean' pattern established earlier in this stream, since accountability does not change, only the office holding the function.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed structural feature: the Economic Stabilization BOARD itself (the multi-member advisory body from EO 9250, prior batch) is preserved and transferred alongside the functions specifically 'for the purpose of advising and consulting with the Director' — its advisory role survives the consolidation rather than being extinguished.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Folds the entire wage/price-stabilization apparatus (Office of Economic Stabilization, its Director's functions, and its advisory Board) into OWMR — a substantial, if not novel-power-creating, institutional consolidation, part of the broader wave of agency-folding this batch (EO 9617, EO 9620) accompanying the immediate postwar reconversion.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The Economic Stabilization Board's advisory role is expressly preserved, not removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Part of the significant late-September 1945 wave of postwar agency consolidations (alongside EO 9617's Department of Labor transfer) marking the rapid institutional simplification of the wartime economic-control apparatus in the weeks immediately following Japan's surrender.

title: Executive Order 9624—Reinstating Wilys R. Peck in the Foreign Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Official text was not published; based on the disclosed title, this is an individualized Foreign Service personnel-reinstatement action of the same narrow, non-discretionary type as other unpublished reinstatement orders in this stream (EO 9457, EO 9558), and is coded conservatively on that basis.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Individualized personnel action; nothing in the disclosed title suggests any discretionary or structural grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is implicated by a personnel reinstatement.

title: Executive Order 9623—Reinstating Leland B. Morris in the Foreign Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Official text was not published; the same conservative treatment as EO 9624 applies.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Individualized personnel action; nothing in the disclosed title suggests any discretionary or structural grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is implicated by a personnel reinstatement.

title: Executive Order 9625—Revoking Executive Order 9294 of January 4, 1943, Further Defining the Functions and Duties of the Office of Defense Transportation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A pure revocation of a prior ODT functions-definition order (outside this batch's coding window); contracts rather than expands authority, part of the broader postwar wind-down of wartime transportation controls.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single named prior order revoked.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9627—Amending Executive Order 9611 of September 10, 1945, Reestablishing the Advisory Board On Just Compensation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow membership-substitution amendment to the already-scored EO 9611 (this batch) Advisory Board on Just Compensation, replacing one unavailable judge with another and resetting the Board's own 60-day operating clock; the Board's genuine independent-judicial-panel structure is entirely preserved.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, named substitute judge, with the Board's original independence-preserving structure (EO 9611) unaffected.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the independent-panel structure scored clean in EO 9611 remains intact.

title: Executive Order 9626—Appointment of the Member and Alternate Member for the United States of the International Military Tribunal Established for the Trial and Punishment of the Major War Criminals of the European Axis

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Appoints two named individuals (Francis Biddle as Member, John J. Parker as Alternate) to fulfill U.S. obligations under a four-power international Charter (the August 8, 1945 agreement among the U.S., UK, USSR, and Provisional French Republic); a multilateral-treaty-anchored appointment, not a unilateral domestic power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, named individuals, multilateral Charter basis, and disclosed compensation-determination channel (Secretary of State).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically monumental despite its narrow textual form: this order appoints the actual U.S. judge (Biddle) and alternate judge (Parker) of the Nuremberg International Military Tribunal, one of the foundational institutions of modern international criminal law — a direct structural consequence of Justice Jackson's earlier prosecutorial designation (EO 9547, this batch).

title: Executive Order 9629—Amending Executive Order 9492, Prescribing Regulations Governing Non-military and Non-naval Transportation On Army and Navy Air Transports

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, single-word amendment extending the already-scored EO 9492 (prior batch) fixed sunset from 'one year' to 'eighteen months'; the underlying disclosed FIXED-DATE sunset structure (one of the cleanest in the stream) is preserved, merely lengthened.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single substitution.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The genuine, fixed-date sunset praised in EO 9492 is retained (now 18 months rather than 12), not eliminated.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9628—Authorizing the Appointment to Positions of Contact Representative, Veterans Administration, Without Regard to the Requirements of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, individualized-criteria civil-service exception for Medal of Honor recipients with veteran's preference, triggered only upon Administrator recommendation; benefits-conferring, not power-concentrating.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective dual-criteria test (Medal of Honor plus veteran's preference), with an Administrator recommendation requirement.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9630—Redistribution of Foreign Economic Functions and Functions With Respect to Surplus Property in Foreign Areas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Terminates the Foreign Economic Administration (established by the already-scored, CRITICAL EO 9380, prior batch, 68.75%) and its Administrator's office, redistributing its functions across FOUR separate, independently-accountable destinations (State, RFC, Commerce, Agriculture) rather than concentrating them in any single successor official — a genuine de-concentration of a formerly CRITICAL apex institution.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Each destination agency is an existing, independently-accountable Cabinet department or corporation; no single new unchecked office is created.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: A complex, multi-directional bundling event: a single order simultaneously redistributes distinct functional categories (Lend-Lease-Act administration, UNRRA participation, liberated-area supply, economic intelligence-gathering, export control, foreign-property disposal, food programs) across four different destination agencies, plus abolishing the Army-Navy Liquidation Commissioner office outright (Sec. 7) — an unusually intricate dissolution, even though the overall direction is de-concentrating.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; if anything, functions move from a single wartime Administrator to established, ordinarily-accountable departments.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Dissolves the Foreign Economic Administration, one of the highest-scoring institutions in the prior batch (EO 9380, CRITICAL on four flags, 68.75%) — one of the most significant single institutional de-concentration events in this stream, marking the formal end of the wartime foreign-economic-coordination apparatus.

title: Executive Order 9631—Termination of the Office of Censorship

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A routine wind-down order (Bureau of the Budget Director given standard liquidation-administration discretion, matching the EO 9471/EO 9562 termination template); narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed fixed-date sunset for the liquidation itself: the Office 'shall continue to function for the purposes of liquidation until the close of business on November 15, 1945,' at which point it and the Director's office 'shall terminate' — an unusually clean, date-certain wind-down.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; Sec. 4 expressly preserves accountability for any 'proceedings instituted, or forfeitures incurred' before the war's end, rather than erasing them.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Terminates the Office of Censorship, established by EO 8985 (Dec. 1941) — independently one of the highest-scoring orders in this entire stream (70%, CRITICAL on multiple flags for its sweeping, largely unchecked wartime communications-interception authority) — making this the single most consequential 'bookend' termination encountered to date, alongside EO 9603's mass seizure-return and EO 9645's Hawaii military-authority termination (this batch).

title: Executive Order 9634—Providing for the Establishment of Fishery Conservation Zones

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow procedural mechanism: the Secretaries of State and Interior are directed to jointly RECOMMEND future zone-establishment orders, not to establish zones unilaterally themselves — implementing machinery for a proclamation, not a self-executing grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed dual-Secretary joint-recommendation requirement, not a single official's fiat.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Implements, alongside the companion Truman Proclamation on coastal fisheries issued the same day, one of the foundational unilateral U.S. assertions of jurisdiction over adjacent ocean resources — a direct historical precursor to the extended fisheries/economic zones that would later be codified internationally through the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea, worth flagging prominently despite the narrow, procedurally-clean text of this particular implementing order.

title: Executive Order 9632—Transfer of Jurisdiction from the War Department to the Department of the Interior Over the Interest of the United States in Certain Mineral Deposits in Jefferson Parish, Louisiana

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, protective jurisdictional transfer (preventing drainage loss of federal mineral royalties) between two departments, with War Department's primary river/harbor jurisdiction expressly preserved (Sec. 3); matches the established Cerritos-Channel-style template (EO 9571, prior batch).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed rationale, historical agreement basis, and preserved War Department primary jurisdiction.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: War Department's primary jurisdiction over river/harbor improvement is expressly preserved, not displaced.

title: Executive Order 9633—Reserving and Placing Certain Resources of the Continental Shelf Under the Control and Jurisdiction of the Secretary of the Interior

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Vests the Secretary of the Interior with administrative jurisdiction over continental-shelf subsoil/seabed resources, but explicitly 'for administrative purposes, pending the enactment of legislation in regard thereto' — a genuinely provisional, Congress-anticipating grant, not an open-ended one.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed savings clause: the order expressly states it 'shall not be deemed to affect the determination by legislation or judicial decree of any issues between the United States and the several states' — preserving both the legislative and judicial resolution channels for the underlying jurisdictional question.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The savings clause is a genuine, disclosed preservation of legislative and judicial review, not a removal of it.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Implements the Truman Proclamation on the Continental Shelf, issued the same day — one of the most consequential unilateral assertions of maritime jurisdiction in modern international law, the direct ancestor of the continental-shelf doctrine later codified in the 1958 Geneva Convention and the UN Convention on the Law of the Sea, and a foundational precedent cited by coastal states worldwide in subsequent resource-sovereignty claims.

title: Executive Order 9635—Organization of the Navy Department and the Naval Establishment

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Secretary of the Navy is granted broad, open-ended internal-reorganization discretion: 'the structure of the organization... shall be such as the Secretary of the Navy may deem appropriate and necessary,' including authority to 'transfer to, from, and among the offices and bureaus of the Navy Department such of their functions and duties... as may be appropriate and necessary.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed non-expansion safeguard: Sec. 9 expressly states 'nothing in this order is intended to modify the statutory authority, duties or responsibilities of the Secretary of the Navy, nor shall it be so construed' — the reorganization redistributes the EXERCISE of existing statutory authority internally rather than creating new substantive power.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he may deem appropriate and necessary' recurs as the operative standard throughout, with no further definition.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The non-expansion safeguard (Sec. 9) is a genuine, disclosed limit.

title: Executive Order 9637—Medal for Merit

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes rules for a statute-created civilian decoration (Medal for Merit), administered through a genuinely independent three-member Board rather than a single official; supersedes the narrower EO 9331 (prior batch) Board.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed multi-member Board structure with State Department recommendation required for foreign-national awards, and Board rules require presidential approval.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9636—Observance by Government Agencies of Holidays Falling on Sundays

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A routine, government-wide Monday-observance rule for holidays falling on Sunday, with detailed objective computation rules for pay/leave purposes; purely administrative.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective rules with defined exceptions for overlapping workweek schedules.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9638—Creating the Civilian Production Administration and Terminating the War Production Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Terminates the War Production Board — the institution behind EO 9024, this stream's first quadruple-CRITICAL order (68.75%), whose Chairman's decisions were expressly 'final' with no superior review — and transfers its functions to a new Civilian Production Administrator who, unlike the WPB Chairman, must 'exercise his functions and powers in accordance with the general policies established by the Director of War Mobilization and Reconversion' — a genuinely NEW, disclosed subordination the predecessor institution never had.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The OWMR Director's policy-setting authority over the new Administrator is a real, disclosed check absent from the WPB's original 'his decisions shall be final' design.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles the entirety of WPB's former functions, powers, and (nearly all) personnel into a single successor Administration in one consolidating transfer.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The new OWMR subordination is a disclosed check added, not removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Terminates the War Production Board, the single highest-profile CRITICAL institution from the early-1942 batch of this stream, and notably subjects its successor to real superior-authority oversight the original never had — both a major structural bookend and a genuine, disclosed accountability improvement over its predecessor.

title: Executive Order 9640—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate the Disputes Between the Railway Express Agency, Inc., and Certain of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this batch: three independent members, fixed 30-day deadline, mutual status-quo requirement.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9641—Sale of Certain Combatant Vessels of the Navy

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-anchored (1883 Act) authorization to sell already-stricken Navy vessels at public sale after a mandatory 30-day advertisement period; routine postwar fleet-drawdown administration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective public-sale procedure with a mandatory advertisement period, a genuine transparency safeguard.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the mandatory advertisement period is itself a disclosed safeguard.

title: Executive Order 9642—Amendment of Executive Order 9356 of June 24, 1943, as Amended, Prescribing Regulations Governing the Furnishing of Clothing in Kind Or Payment of Cash Allowances in Lieu Thereof to Enlisted Personnel of the Navy, the Coast Guard, the Naval Reserve, and the Coast Guard Reserve

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A further narrow, fixed-rate clothing-allowance schedule update; purely arithmetic/administrative, the fourth such amendment to EO 9356 across this and the prior batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed dollar figures.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9643—Transferring Certain Personnel, Records, Property, and Funds of the Department of Commerce, With Resepct to Surplus Property, to the Reconstruction Finance Corporation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A statutorily-anchored jurisdictional transfer (implementing a Surplus Property Administrator designation under the Surplus Property Act of 1944) from Commerce to RFC — the same disposal-agency-reassignment mechanism as EO 9541 (this batch, Treasury to Commerce), now moving one step further; no new discretionary power is created.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed statutory basis and designation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; implements the Surplus Property Administrator's own prior regulatory designation.

title: Executive Order 9644—Authorizing the Civil Service Commission to Confer a Competitive Classfied Civil-service Status Upon Certain Disabled Veterans

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, objective-criteria civil-service status grant for disabled veterans (10%+ service-connected disability) with defined trial-period requirements; benefits-conferring, not power-concentrating.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, multi-part objective eligibility test with certification and trial-period requirements.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9645—Termination of the Authority Conferred By Executive Order No. 9489 of October 18, 1944

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A pure termination order ending the military commander's Hawaii military-area/exclusion authority (EO 9489, prior batch, CRITICAL on five flags, tied the all-time-high 75%); narrows rather than expands executive power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed factual predicate for termination: 'the exercise of the authority conferred... is no longer necessary in the interest of the national defense.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: A significant, disclosed limitation on this order's remedial effect: 'such termination shall not operate to affect any action heretofore accomplished... nor shall such termination operate to affect any offenses heretofore committed or any conviction had or penalty incurred because of violations of the provisions of any public proclamations, orders, regulations, restrictions, or prohibitions issued by the said military commander.' This forecloses any retroactive review of, or remedy for, actions taken under EO 9489's fully self-graded, unreviewed exclusion/detention authority — convictions and penalties imposed under that CRITICAL-scored regime remain legally undisturbed, with no review mechanism created by this termination.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Marks the formal termination of the Hawaii military-area/exclusion authority that tied this stream's all-time-high structural-weight score (EO 9489, 75%) — an important structural bookend, though tempered by the Flag 7 finding above: the order closes the authority prospectively without addressing the legal status of its past exercise.

title: Executive Order 9648—Discontinuing the Matagorda Bay Defensive Sea Area

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A pure revocation of a prior wartime defensive-sea-area designation (EO 9168, outside this batch's coding window); narrows rather than expands authority, part of the broader postwar wind-down of coastal military-security zones.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single named prior order discontinued.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9646—Coat of Arms, Seal, and Flag of the President of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A purely symbolic, ceremonial-heraldry specification order (coat of arms, seal, presidential flag design); no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, detailed design specification.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9647—Regulations Relating to the Giving of Public Notice and the Presentation of Views in Connection With Foreign Trade Agreements

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Establishes a genuine public notice-and-comment mechanism for foreign trade agreement negotiations (30-day advance Federal Register notice, a multi-agency Committee for Reciprocity Information to receive public views); this order EXPANDS procedural transparency and public participation rather than concentrating discretionary power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed multi-agency committee structure (Tariff Commission, State, Treasury, War, Navy, Agriculture, Commerce) with a chairman designated by the Secretary of State, and a mandatory 30-day advance public notice requirement — real, disclosed procedural safeguards.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, a public-participation oversight mechanism.

title: Executive Order 9649—Termination of the Office of Fishery Coordination

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A routine wind-down order terminating a wartime coordination office and reassigning residual food-related functions to the Department of the Interior's existing Fish and Wildlife Service; narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, existing-agency reassignment.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9650—Discontinuing Portland, Maine; Portsmouth, New Hampshire; Boston, Massachusetts; Narragansett Bay; New London, Connecticut; New York Harbor; Delaware Bay and River; Chesapeake Bay-norfolk; Charleston Harbor; and Buzzards Bay and Vineyard Sound Defensive Sea Areas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A mass discontinuation of ten East Coast wartime defensive-sea-area designations in a single order; narrows rather than expands authority, part of the broader postwar wind-down of coastal military-security zones.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, each of the ten discontinued designations individually cited to its establishing order.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; this order removes ten restrictions rather than removing oversight of them.

title: Executive Order 9651—Amending Executive Order 9599, Providing for Assistance to Expanded Production and Continued Stablization of the National Economy During the Transition from War to Peace, and for the Orderly Modification of Wartime Controls Over Prices, Wages, Materials and Facilities

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A diff-aware, substantive amendment to the already-scored EO 9599 (this batch, 56.25%): formally designates the 'Stabilization Administrator' (per EO 9620, this batch) as the approving official for wage/salary increases and defines three specific approval categories, continuing the apex-coordinator wage-decontrol design established in EO 9599's Title IV.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Retains and specifies multiple genuine, disclosed checks: increases must be 'found by the National War Labor Board... to be necessary,' new Sec. 1's three categories are defined by objective comparative standards (e.g., a cost-of-living-index comparison since January 1941), and new Sec. 3 gives the Price Administrator a defined 'test period' (six months, absent exceptional cases) before factoring a wage increase into price-ceiling determinations — a real, disclosed procedural sequence, not unbounded discretion.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: A single coherent amendment to the already-bundled EO 9599 framework; no new distinct bundling is introduced beyond what EO 9599 itself already reflects.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: New Sec. 1's residual clause — the Stabilization Administrator 'may define additional classes of wage or salary increases... as necessary' — retains open-ended definitional authority.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The NWLB necessity-finding requirement and the Price Administrator's defined test period are genuine, disclosed checks, not ones removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Operationalizes rather than independently expands EO 9599's already-flagged historical significance; no additional distinct second-order effect beyond what EO 9599 itself represents.

title: Executive Order 9652—Authorizing the Appointment of Certain Personnel in the Department of State

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, defined-class appointment authority for military personnel released from active duty who were already working on functions recently transferred to State (EO 9621/EO 9630, this batch); bounded by objective eligibility criteria tied to specific prior transfer orders.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective eligibility criteria tied to named prior orders, subject to CSC noncompetitive-test prescription.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; CSC's testing role is preserved.

title: Executive Order 9653—Amending Schedules a and B of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extensive but purely administrative set of updates to Civil Service Rules exemption schedules (salary thresholds, position descriptions) across many narrow position categories; no discretionary power grant, and several changes require 'prior approval of the Commission' for use, a disclosed safeguard.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, itemized, objective salary/position criteria throughout.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; several provisions explicitly require Commission approval.

title: Executive Order 9655—Regulations Relating to Commissioned Officers and Employees of the Public Health Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extraordinarily long and detailed comprehensive regulatory code implementing the Public Health Service Act of 1944 (appointment, promotion, retirement, discipline, uniforms); despite its scale, authority for the most consequential quasi-judicial actions (disability retirement, disciplinary separation, promotion) is consistently vested in MULTI-OFFICER BOARDS with defined composition requirements (e.g., 'three or more officers, the majority of whom... shall be of the same profession'), not a single unchecked official.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: About as far from an unchecked, vague grant as an order in this stream gets: detailed, objective, numerically-specified criteria throughout (minimum ratings, required service years, presumption rules for disability determinations), with disclosed appeal-like structures (Administrator review of Surgeon General orders, board re-investigation authority).

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Length and comprehensiveness notwithstanding, this is a single coherent statutory-implementation exercise (PHS Commissioned Corps personnel administration), matching the treatment given EO 9578's comparably detailed CSC pay regulations (prior batch).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; multi-officer boards and Administrator-level review are built into nearly every consequential determination.

title: Executive Order 9656—Transfer of Shipbuilding Stabilization Committee

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow reorganizational transfer of a wage-stabilization committee from the newly-created Civilian Production Administration (this batch) to the Department of Labor, an already-accountable Cabinet department; consistent with the 'reorganization to accountable official reads clean' pattern.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed transfer scope, with the Secretary of Labor filling future Committee vacancies under the existing appointive-membership structure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9657—Providing for the Representation of the United States in the Observation of Elections in Greece

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Designates a named individual (Henry F. Grady) to lead a multinational election-observation delegation, grounded in a disclosed multilateral commitment (the February 1945 Crimea Conference) and a Greek government invitation; a personnel-designation and inter-agency-cooperation-authorization instrument, not a domestic power-concentration grant, with expenditures bounded by 'appropriate appropriations now or hereafter available.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed international basis, named representative, and bounded fiscal authority (up to 500 military personnel, expressly capped).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically notable as one of the earliest postwar U.S. election-observation missions, directly implementing the Crimea Conference's Declaration on Liberated Europe commitment to free elections — a precursor to the extensive election-monitoring diplomacy the U.S. would engage in throughout the Cold War and beyond.

title: Executive Order 9658—Possession, Control, and Operation of the Transportation System, Plants, and Facilities of the Capital Transit Complany, Washington, District of Columbia

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of the Office of Defense Transportation is granted open-ended seizure/operation authority — 'in such a manner as he may deem necessary to carry out the provisions, and accomplish the purposes of this order' — over the entire public transit system of the national capital.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; the Director alone certifies the triggering threat and, per Sec. 4, alone determines termination — a nominal, self-graded check.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: A single named transit company; standard single-entity scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such a manner as he may deem necessary' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 4's termination trigger is entirely self-judged with no fixed date and no 60-day outer cap — the same self-perpetuation risk pattern as EO 9536/9548/9554/9602 (this and prior batch).

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed sunset; termination is wholly contingent on the Director's own, unreviewed determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 3 explicitly bars, 'except with the prior written consent of the Director,' any 'attachment by mesne process, garnishment, execution, or otherwise' against property in the Director's possession — the same direct creditor/judicial-remedy preemption clause now appearing for the FOURTH time in this stream (EO 9412, EO 9462, EO 9554, EO 9602), confirming it as a durable, recurring seizure-power feature rather than a one-off.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Uniquely among this stream's transit/motor-carrier seizures, this order targets the public transportation system of Washington, D.C. itself — the seat of the federal government — giving it distinctive national-symbolic and operational visibility beyond its otherwise-modest single-entity scope.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 6: the same 'Director... may request the Secretary of War to furnish protection... equipment, manpower, and other facilities or services' cross-agency assistance clause as the other Office of Defense Transportation seizure orders this stream.

title: Executive Order 9659—Extension of Trust Periods On Indian Lands Expiring During Calendar Year 1946

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The 1946 iteration of the same protective, beneficiary-favoring blanket trust-period extension (25 years) already scored for EO 9500 (prior batch), with the same congressional-reservation carve-out preserved; extends an existing protective status, not a new discretionary grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed 25-year extension term with the same disclosed congressional-reservation carve-out.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The order preserves rather than displaces Congress's own reserved authority.

title: Executive Order 9660—Conferring Certain Authority Upon the Chief of Counsel in the Preparation and Prosectution of Charges of War Crimes Against the Major Leaders of Japan and Their Principal Agents and Accessories

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Designates Joseph B. Keenan as Chief of Counsel for the prosecution of Japanese war criminals, authorized to select personnel and receive inter-agency/foreign-government assistance; the same personnel-designation and cooperation-authorization structure as EO 9547 (this batch, Justice Jackson/Nuremberg), not a domestic power-concentration grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope, with expenditures bounded by available appropriations.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: The direct Pacific-theater counterpart to EO 9547's Nuremberg designation: this order founds the prosecutorial leadership of the Tokyo International Military Tribunal, another foundational institution of modern international criminal law, again scoring modestly on this rubric's domestic-power-concentration terms despite its outsized historical significance.

title: Executive Order 9662—Restricting Competition in Certain Civil-service Examinations to Veterans

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, veterans'-preference civil-service restriction for a single position class (Correctional Officer, Bureau of Prisons), matching the established EO 9589/EO 9662-family template (statute-anchored, defined duration tied to war termination plus five years).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single position class and defined statutory duration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9663—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Texas & New Orleans Railway Company and Hospital Association of the Southern Pacific Lines in Texas and Lousiana, and Certain of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this batch: three independent members, fixed 30-day deadline, mutual status-quo requirement — here covering a dispute involving twelve separate labor organizations.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9664—Continuing the Work of the Fair Employment Practice Committee

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Continues, rather than expands, the FEPC's existing investigative/recommendatory authority (originally EO 8802, amended by EO 8823 and EO 9346, all outside this batch's coding window), expressly bounded 'for the period and subject to the conditions stated in the National War Agencies Appropriation Act, 1946.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed appropriations-act-tied duration limitation, not an open-ended continuation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the appropriations-tied sunset is itself a disclosed constraint.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Extends the historically foundational FEPC (previously flagged CRITICAL on this flag for EO 9346, prior batch, as a precursor to postwar civil-rights employment law) into the reconversion era with an expanded mandate to investigate discrimination in the transition to a peacetime economy — though the Committee's continuation via appropriations rider rather than permanent legislation foreshadows its ultimate 1946 lapse without a lasting statutory FEPC, a historically significant near-miss worth noting.

title: Executive Order 9666—Directing the Return of the Coast Guard to the Treasury Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Returns the Coast Guard to its peacetime home department (Treasury) after more than four years operating under Navy command (EO 8929, outside this batch's coding window, now revoked); narrows rather than expands executive control, part of the broader postwar demobilization wave.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed mutual-agreement structure: any continued Navy control of specific vessels/functions beyond January 1, 1946 requires agreement by BOTH the Secretary of the Treasury AND the Secretary of the Navy — a real bilateral, not unilateral, continuation mechanism.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The bilateral-agreement structure is a genuine, disclosed check, not one removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A symbolically and operationally significant demobilization milestone — the Coast Guard's formal return to civilian control after over four years as part of the wartime Navy, part of the broader wave of wartime-authority unwinding running through this batch (alongside EO 9603, EO 9631, EO 9638, EO 9645).

title: Executive Order 9667—Revoking Executive Order 9074 of February, 25, 1942, Directing the Secretary of the Navy to Take Action Necessary to Protect Vessels, Harbors, Ports and Waterfront Facilities

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A pure revocation of a prior wartime waterfront-protection authorization (outside this batch's coding window); narrows rather than expands authority, part of the broader postwar wind-down.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single named prior order revoked.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9668—Amending Subdivision Iii of Schedule a of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical clarification of a civil-service exemption category (insolvent-bank receivers and their employees, funded from institution funds rather than appropriations); administrative housekeeping.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective category definition.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9669—Transfer of Air-navigation Facilities and Functions in Iran from the War Department to the Administrator of Civil Aeronautics

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, geographically-bounded transfer of specific air-navigation facilities (Tehran, Abadan, Jask) from the War Department to a civilian aviation regulator; a demobilization-era transfer FROM military TO civilian control, the protective direction.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, itemized facility list and funding-transfer mechanism (Bureau of the Budget Director determination).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; this order shifts control toward, not away from, civilian regulatory oversight.

title: Executive Order 9670—Establishing the Tennessee National Wildlife Refuge

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow conservation-designation order implementing a disclosed bilateral agreement (TVA-Interior, Nov. 29, 1945) under the Migratory Bird Conservation Act; a protective land-use designation, not a discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed agreement basis and statutory purpose.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9671—Restoring Certain Lands to the Territory of Hawaii

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A multi-parcel but narrowly bounded land restoration (several historical Hawaii Department of Agriculture experiment-station parcels) to Territorial control, with each parcel individually surveyed and described; narrows rather than expands federal control.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, detailed metes-and-bounds descriptions for each of six parcels.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order restores rather than removes territorial jurisdiction.

title: Executive Order 9673—Transfer of Certain Production Research and Development Functions from the Civilian Production Administration to the Department of Commerce

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow reorganizational transfer of a specific research-and-development office from CPA to Commerce, an already-accountable Cabinet department; existing unliquidated research contracts are expressly excluded from the transfer.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope, with the Bureau of the Budget Director determining necessary implementing measures.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9675—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate the Disputes Between the St. Louis-San Francisco Railway Company and the St. Louis, San Francisco and Texas Railway Company, Carriers, and Certain of Their Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this batch: three independent members, fixed 30-day deadline, mutual status-quo requirement.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9678—Amendment of Executive Order No. 8743 of April 23, 1941, Extending the Classified Civil Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical amendment expanding the reinstatement window and adding a department/agency-head recommendation-and-certification requirement for returning veterans acquiring classified status; a benefits-clarifying, not power-concentrating, change.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, multi-part objective eligibility test (discharge status, filing deadline, head-of-department certification of merit, noncompetitive examination).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9676—Terminal Date for Filing Application for Non-Necessity Certificate Under Section 124(d) of the Internal Revenue Code

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Adds a fixed filing deadline (February 15, 1946) to an existing certificate-application regulation; narrows rather than expands discretion by imposing a hard cutoff.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed date.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: This order itself creates a genuine, fixed-date procedural sunset.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9677—Authorizing the Appointment of Certain Persons at the Home of Franklin D. Roosevelt National Historic Site, National Park Service, Without Regard to the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Not published in the Federal Register; coded conservatively by title-pattern-matching against the many similar narrow, site-specific civil-service-exemption appointment orders in this stream (e.g., EO 9628, this batch), which consistently score 0%.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Consistent with the established pattern for comparable published orders of this type.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No indication of oversight removal.

title: Executive Order 9679—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9547 of May 2, 1945, Entitled "Providing for Representation of the United States in Preparing and Prosecuting Charges of Atrocities and War Crimes Against Leaders of the European Axis Powers and Their Principal Agents and Accessories"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A diff-aware, substantive amendment to the already-scored EO 9547 (prior batch): broadens the Chief of Counsel's prosecutorial authority beyond the international-tribunal 'Case No. 1' proceeding to reach 'other Axis adherents, including but not limited to cases against members of groups and organizations declared criminal' before U.S. military/occupation tribunals — a genuine expansion of an already-designated official's reach.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Includes a genuine, disclosed succession mechanism (Sec. 3): upon the present Representative's vacation of office, future authority vests in a 'Chief of Counsel for War Crimes' appointed by the United States Military Governor for Germany — an externally-anchored appointment process, not an open-ended personal grant.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: A single coherent amendment to the existing EO 9547 framework.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Extends the historically significant Nuremberg-prosecution apparatus established by EO 9547 into the broader U.S. occupation-tribunal system, continuing this stream's recurring pattern of modest structural scores for historically foundational war-crimes-prosecution orders.

title: Executive Order 9680—Abolishing the United States of America Typhus Commission

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A routine wind-down order abolishing a wartime disease-control commission as of a fixed future date (June 30, 1946), based on a disclosed factual finding that its mission will soon be complete; narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed fixed-date abolition and liquidation-supervision assignment to the Secretary of War.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9682—Providing for the Furnishing of Information and Assistance to the Joint Anglo-American Committee of Inquiry On Jewish Problems in Palestine and Europe

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An information-sharing and inter-agency assistance authorization for an international inquiry committee, not a domestic power-concentration grant; expressly bounded by a confidentiality carve-out.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed carve-out: departments need not disclose records 'the disclosure of which would be prejudicial to the interests of the United States,' but must furnish 'a statement justifying the withholding' — a real, disclosed accountability mechanism for any refusal.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the justification requirement for withholding is itself a disclosed check.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Historically significant as the mechanism supporting the Anglo-American Committee of Inquiry, whose findings and recommendations directly shaped the postwar Palestine and Jewish-refugee debate — a foundational episode in the lead-up to the 1947 UN Partition Plan and Israel's founding, despite this implementing order's narrow, procedurally-clean text.

title: Executive Order 9683—Restoring Limitations Upon Punishments for Certain Violations of Articles of War 58, 59, 61 and 86

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Restores normal, statute-contemplated peacetime limitations on court-martial punishments for desertion, AWOL, and related offenses that had been suspended during the war (EO 9048/EO 9267, outside this batch's coding window); the corpus text is truncated after 'it is deemed no longer necessary,' but the title and available text confirm a restorative, narrowing action.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A restoration of standard limitations is itself a disclosed accountability improvement over the wartime suspension.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; this order restores a punishment ceiling that constrains, rather than expands, military discretion.

title: Executive Order 9684—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate Disputes Between the Indiana Harbor Belt Railroad, Chicago Junction Railway (the C. R. & I. R. R. Co., Lessee) and Chicago River and Indiana Railroad Company, Carriers, and Certain of Their Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9685—Authorizing the Secretary of Agriculture to Take Possession of and Operate Certain Plants and Facilities Used in the Production, Processing, Transportation, Sale and Distribution of Livestock, Meat, Meat Products and By-Products

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Secretary of Agriculture is granted open-ended possession/operation authority — 'to operate or to arrange for the use and operation of such plants and facilities in any manner that he deems necessary in the interest of the war effort' — across a massive nationwide list of meatpacking plants (Armour, Swift, Cudahy, Wilson, and dozens of smaller packers).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed check: existing terms of employment continue 'until and unless the duly authorized federal agencies shall, with the approval of the President, otherwise direct' — any change requires the President's own approval, a real check by a higher accountable official rather than the Secretary's unilateral discretion.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: A single coherent industry-wide seizure (meatpacking); the extensive plant list reflects scope, not multi-purpose bundling.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In any manner that he deems necessary' is an open-ended operative standard.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 7 provides a genuine, disclosed 60-day outer cap on possession after the Secretary determines pre-interruption productive efficiency has been restored — not an open-ended, self-perpetuating trigger.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The 60-day cap functions as a real, if determination-triggered, sunset provision.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No judicial-remedy-bar or similar oversight-preemption clause is present in this order, unlike the Office of Defense Transportation seizure template.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A routine wartime-authority plant seizure matching the standard template; no additional distinct historical significance beyond the pattern already established for this order family.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 6: broad 'All Federal agencies... shall cooperate with the Secretary of Agriculture to the fullest extent of their authority' cross-agency cooperation clause, plus a Secretary of War protection-assistance-on-request provision (Sec. 5).

title: Executive Order 9686—Providing for the Administration By the Housing Expediter of Certain Functions Relating to Housing

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants the Housing Expediter broad cross-agency directive authority: 'Issue such orders, regulations, or directives to other executive agencies as may be necessary... Each executive agency shall carry out without delay the orders, regulations, or directives of the Housing Expediter.'

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No independent board or external check is specified for the Expediter's directive-issuing power; accountability runs only through the ordinary OWMR/presidential chain of command — a nominal, non-binding check.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles plan-formulation authority, binding cross-agency directive power, and legislative-recommendation authority into a single office in one order.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As may be necessary' recurs as the operative standard for both plan formulation (Sec. 1(a)) and cross-agency directives (Sec. 1(b)).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No explicit oversight-preemption clause (e.g., a judicial-remedy bar) is present.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Establishes the postwar Housing Expediter's cross-agency coordinating authority over the acute veteran-housing shortage — a historically significant postwar institutional creation with substantial downstream effects on national housing policy, though this order itself does not yet specify the more extensive rent-control and construction-priority powers the office would later exercise.

title: Executive Order 9687—Revoking Executive Orders Concerning Foreign Service Pay Adjustment

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A technical revocation of a series of obsolete Depression/wartime-era currency-fluctuation pay-adjustment orders (1938-1944), effective at a fixed future pay-period date; narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, itemized list of revoked prior orders and fixed effective date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9688—Amending Schedule B of the Civil Service Rules

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, single-paragraph civil-service exemption for positions assigned exclusively to Navy Communications Intelligence activities; a routine national-security-sensitive personnel exemption, standard in this stream.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, narrow category definition.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9689—Consolidation of Surplus Property Functions

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Consolidates fragmented wartime surplus-property-disposal functions (Surplus Property Administration, War Assets Corporation, RFC) into a single new War Assets Administration headed by a Senate-confirmed Administrator — a genuine consolidation, but into a position subject to Advice and Consent, an accountability mechanism many wartime emergency offices lacked.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed external check: the War Assets Administrator is 'appointed by the President by and with the advice and consent of the Senate' (Sec. 3(a)).

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles the functions of three separate predecessor bodies (Surplus Property Administration, War Assets Corporation, relevant RFC functions) into a single new agency in one consolidating order, with foreign-area functions separately routed to State.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; Senate confirmation is an added, disclosed check.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Establishes the War Assets Administration, the primary postwar mechanism for disposing of an enormous volume of wartime surplus property (aircraft, vehicles, equipment, real estate) — a historically significant institutional creation with substantial downstream economic effects on postwar markets and veteran-benefit sales programs.

title: Executive Order 9690—Amending Executive Order 9685 Authorizing the Secretary of Agriculture to Take Possession of and Operate Certain Plants and Facilities Used in the Production, Processing, Transportation, Sale and Distribution of Livestock Meat, Meat Products and By-Products

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, single-company addition (Los Angeles Union Stockyards Company) to the already-scored EO 9685 seizure list, based on a disclosed factual finding; not an independent expansion of the underlying authority's structure.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single named addition with stated factual basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; inherits EO 9685's existing structure unchanged.

title: Executive Order 9692—Amending Executive Order 9472 of August 29, 1944, Establishing Certain Awards for the Merchant Marine

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, symbolic amendment adding a Merchant Marine Honorable Service Button and a design-non-duplication rule; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9693—Possession, Control, and Operation of the Transportation Systems, Plants, and Facilities of Certain Towing and Transportation Companies Operating in New York Harbor and Contiguous Waters

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of the Office of Defense Transportation is granted open-ended seizure/operation authority — 'in such manner as he may deem necessary' — over roughly ninety named towing and lighterage companies operating in New York Harbor.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; the Director alone certifies the triggering threat and, per Sec. 4, alone determines termination — a nominal, self-graded check.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: A single coherent industry seizure (harbor towing/transportation); the large company list reflects scope, not multi-purpose bundling.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such manner as he may deem necessary' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 4's termination trigger is entirely self-judged with no fixed date and no outer cap — the same self-perpetuation risk pattern recurring throughout this batch's ODT seizure orders.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed sunset; termination is wholly contingent on the Director's own, unreviewed determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 3 again bars, absent the Director's prior written consent, any 'attachment by mesne process, garnishment, execution, or otherwise' against seized property — the same direct creditor/judicial-remedy preemption clause appearing for a sixth time in this stream (EO 9412, EO 9462, EO 9554, EO 9602, EO 9658, now EO 9693), further confirming it as a durable, deliberately-reused seizure-power design.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Notably, this order was signed nearly six months after V-J Day (September 2, 1945) yet still invokes 'the war effort will be unduly impeded and delayed by such interruptions' as its operative statutory justification for seizure under the Selective Training and Service Act — a clear illustration of nominally wartime statutory authority persisting deep into the reconversion period, even as the seizure itself follows the standard, non-escalating single-industry template.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 6: Secretary of War/Navy protection-and-assistance-on-request cross-agency cooperation clause, identical in structure to the other ODT seizure orders.

title: Executive Order 9694—Inspection of Income, Excess-profits, and Declared Value Excess-profits Tax Returns By the War Contracts Price Adjustment Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An oversight-enhancing tax-return inspection authorization for a contract price-review board, consistent with the recurring pattern of such orders in this stream (always ABSENT across all flags: enables verification/enforcement rather than concentrating discretionary power).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, subject to Treasury-decision conditions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order enables oversight rather than removing it.

title: Executive Order 9695—Revocation of Executive Order No. 9243 of September 12, 1942, Providing for the Transfer and Release of Federal Personnel

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A wind-down revocation of a wartime personnel-transfer/release order, with an explicit, disclosed savings clause preserving reemployment rights already acquired under related prior orders; narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed savings clause (para. 1) and CSC implementation authority to preserve existing rights (para. 2).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9696—Reinstating Monnett B. Davis in the Foreign Service of the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Not published in the Federal Register; coded conservatively by title-pattern-matching against similar narrow, named-individual reinstatement/appointment orders in this stream, which consistently score 0%.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Consistent with the established pattern for comparable published orders of this type.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No indication of oversight removal.

title: Executive Order 9697—Providing for the Continued Stabilization of the National Economy During the Transition from War to Peace

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Vests the Stabilization Administrator (Office of War Mobilization and Reconversion) with sweeping authority to 'issue such orders and directives as may be necessary, in his judgment, to carry out the purposes of this order' (Sec. 4) — continuing and deepening the apex-coordinator design established across EO 9599/EO 9620/EO 9651 (this batch).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Largely nominal: the National Wage Stabilization Board retains a formal wage-increase-approval role (Sec. 3(a)), but the Stabilization Administrator can establish 'special standards' with only his own approval (Sec. 3(a)) and exempt whole classes of increases he judges to have 'no unstabilizing consequences' (Sec. 3(b)); the Price Administrator's hardship-adjustment findings (Sec. 2) are entirely self-determined with no external board — a nominal, non-binding check overall.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles wage-approval standards, price-ceiling hardship-adjustment formulas, government-procurement cost-basis rules, and arbitration-award conformity requirements across OPA, the National Wage Stabilization Board, and the Stabilization Administrator's jurisdiction simultaneously in a single order — cross-agency policy bundling matching or exceeding EO 9599's scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As may be necessary, in his judgment' recurs as the Administrator's operative standard throughout (Secs. 2(c), 3(b), 4).

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Framed as necessary 'for the duration of the existing emergency' — signed over five months after V-J Day with no fixed termination date specified in this order, continuing the recurring zombie-emergency pattern tied to open-ended wartime proclamations.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed sunset; the order's operative duration is tied to the undefined 'existing emergency.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No judicial-remedy-bar or similar oversight-preemption clause is present.

flags.name: Third-Party Incentive Gaps
flags.justification: Sec. 2(b)'s formula guarantees industries in 'hardship' an adjustment sufficient to earn 'an average rate of profit equal... to the rate of return on net worth earned... in the peacetime base period' — a disclosed but generous profit-protection mechanism for regulated industries, a third-party incentive consideration.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
