flags.justification: Represents the most comprehensive, detailed rewrite of the postwar wage-price stabilization framework in this stream, continuing the Reconversion-era apex-coordinator design (EO 9599, EO 9620, EO 9651) with substantially increased complexity — historically significant as a key link in the stabilization program's chain toward its eventual mid-1946 political and administrative collapse.

title: Executive Order 9698—Designating Public International Organizations Entitled to Enjoy Certain Privileges, Exemptions, and Immunities

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-implementing designation (International Organizations Immunities Act, Dec. 1945) with a disclosed, State Department-administered application process for any future organizations.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed application/recommendation procedure through the Secretary of State to the President.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Notable as one of the first formal U.S. domestic-law recognitions of the newly founded United Nations (chartered June 1945), alongside the Food and Agriculture Organization, International Labor Organization, Pan American Union, and UNRRA — a marker of the emerging postwar international institutional order, though the order itself is a narrow, procedurally clean implementation of a 1945 congressional immunities statute.

title: Executive Order 9699—Re-establishing the Office of Economic Stabilization

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Restores a broad, multi-member interagency and public-representative Board structure (Sec. 2) in place of the narrower single-Administrator arrangement created by EO 9620 (this batch) — a genuine de-concentration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed multi-stakeholder Economic Stabilization Board: multiple Cabinet secretaries, the Federal Reserve Board chairman, the SEC chairman, the Price Administrator, the Wage Stabilization Board chairman, plus two representatives each of labor, management, and farmers.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order restores rather than removes an oversight structure.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Marks a notable institutional reversal within the postwar stabilization apparatus, restoring the broader multi-stakeholder Economic Stabilization Board (originally EO 9250, prior batch) that had been effectively superseded by the narrower single-Administrator design of EO 9620 (this batch) — illustrating the stabilization program's institutional volatility in the months leading up to its eventual mid-1946 political unraveling.

title: Executive Order 9700—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate the Disputes Between the Texas & New Orleans Railroad Company, Carrier, and Certain of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9701—Providing for the Reservation of Rights to Fissionable Materials in Lands Owned By the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The reservation-of-minerals mechanism operates through disclosed application windows integrated into standard public-land law procedures (homestead, desert-land, small-tract acts), not open unilateral executive discretion; the Secretary of the Interior's authority to WAIVE the reservation requirement (Sec. 2) where no substantial deposits are likely is itself a specific, bounded, disclosed exception rather than an expansion of power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Detailed, objective, multi-step disclosed procedures throughout: fixed timelines (28-day, 90-day, and 20-day priority windows), veteran-preference categories, and standard public-land-office adjudication.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Supersedes EO 9613 (prior batch, flagged CRITICAL for historic atomic-age significance) with a more comprehensive, permanently-codified fissionable-materials reservation system integrated into ordinary public-land law — part of the foundational domestic legal architecture, predating the Atomic Energy Act of 1946, that reserved nuclear raw materials to federal control, a foundational act of the nuclear age with lasting international-security significance despite this order's clean, procedurally-bounded design.

title: Executive Order 9702—Creating an Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Alton Railroad Company and Other Carriers, and Certain of Their Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this batch, here covering an extraordinarily long list of carriers spanning essentially the entire national rail system (Western, Eastern, Southeastern regions, plus numerous short lines); the scale of the carrier list does not alter the underlying balanced, tripartite, fixed-deadline structure.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9703—Regulations Relating to the Medical Care of Certain Personnel of the Coast Guard, Coast and Geodetic Survey, Public Health Service, and Former Lighthouse Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extensive but purely administrative medical-benefits regulation implementing the Public Health Service Act of 1944; eligibility, facility-use, and hospitalization-cost rules are all objectively defined by category and status, matching the established pattern for detailed PHS/Coast Guard benefits regulations (e.g., EO 9655, this batch).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective, multi-part eligibility criteria throughout, with defined reporting requirements (Sec. 4(c), (e)) for any non-Service-facility treatment.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; reporting requirements for exceptional treatment are themselves disclosed checks.

title: Executive Order 9704—Functions of the American Battle Monuments Commission

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow grant of design/regulatory-approval authority over overseas war memorials to the American Battle Monuments Commission, an already-existing, already-accountable multi-member commission (established by Congress in 1923); not a new concentration of unilateral power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The Commission's existing multi-member structure is the disclosed accountability mechanism.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9705—Delegating Certain Functions and Authority Under the Agricultural Marketing Agreement Act of 1937 to the Economic Stabilization Director

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow statutory delegation of marketing-order approval authority to an already-designated official (the Economic Stabilization Director, re-established by EO 9699, this batch), with a disclosed retroactive ratification of past actions taken under the interim EO 9620 arrangement.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed delegation and ratification clause.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9706—Amending Executive Order No. 9265 of November 6, 1942, Establishing the American, European-African-Middle Eastern and Asiatic-Pacific Campaign Medals

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, symbolic amendment fixing cutoff dates for wartime campaign-medal eligibility; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed dates.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9707—Amendment of the Executive Order of January 31, 1946 Entitled "Consolidation of Surplus Property Functions"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical amendment to EO 9689's (this batch) salary-classification rules, plus a temporary military-personnel-detail authorization for the War Assets Corporation/Administration; no new discretionary power beyond the already-scored EO 9689 framework.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective classification and detail-certification criteria.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9708—Specifying Communicable Diseases for the Purpose of Regulations Providing for the Apprehension, Detention, or Conditional Release of Individuals to Prevent the Introduction, Transmission, or Spread of Communicable Diseases

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Detention authority is statutorily bounded: the Public Health Service Act requires a specific presidential designation of covered diseases via Executive Order, following a joint recommendation from the National Advisory Health Council and Surgeon General, rather than open executive discretion to detain for any reason.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed dual-recommendation requirement (National Advisory Health Council AND Surgeon General) precedes the designation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the statute itself requires this presidential-designation step as a check on the Surgeon General's regulatory power.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Worth flagging for historical context: several listed diseases (notably venereal diseases and trachoma) were historically invoked, including in immigration-exclusion contexts, in ways that could function as pretexts for discriminatory detention of certain populations; this order's text itself is narrow, disease-list-based, and shows no indication of discriminatory intent, but the potential for disparate downstream application is worth noting as a second-order consideration.

title: Executive Order 9709—Providing Interim Arrangements Respecting Certain Air Navigation Facilities Abroad

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Transfers specific overseas air-navigation facilities from War/Navy to the civilian Department of Commerce, subject to a disclosed four-part bounding test (no longer needed for military purposes, useful for civil aviation, fundable from existing Commerce appropriations); a demobilization-era transfer FROM military TO civilian control, and supersedes the narrower EO 9669 (prior batch).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed four-part transfer test, plus a disclosed State Department collaboration/approval requirement for foreign-territory arrangements (Sec. 2).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; this order shifts control toward, not away from, civilian regulatory oversight, and preserves Coast Guard loran-facility authority unchanged (Sec. 7).

title: Executive Order 9710—Terminating the Office of Inter-American Affairs and Transferring Certain of Its Functions

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Terminates the wartime Office of Inter-American Affairs (Nelson Rockefeller's cultural-diplomacy and propaganda coordination agency), transferring its remaining corporate functions to the Secretary of State, an already-accountable Cabinet official; narrows rather than expands authority, with a fixed effective date (May 20, 1946).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope and fixed effective date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9711—Providing Reemployment Benefits for Federal Civilian Employees Who Enter Civilian Service With the War Or Navy Departments in Occupied Areas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, benefits-conferring reemployment-rights guarantee for federal employees and veterans entering civilian occupation-administration service; objective eligibility criteria (90-day application windows, satisfactory-service certification) bound the grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, multi-part objective eligibility test requiring War/Navy Department certification of satisfactory, non-delinquent service.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9712—Amendment of Executive Order No. 8743 of April 23, 1941, Extending the Classified Civil Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical amendment adding a cross-agency reinstatement option for returning veterans (subsection (b)), building on EO 9678's already-scored amendment; benefits-clarifying, not power-concentrating.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective eligibility test (honorable discharge, noncompetitive examination) requiring both employee application and department-head request.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9713—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Chicago, Rock Island & Pacific Railway Company and Certain of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9714—Amending Executive Order No. 9492, as Amended, Prescribing Regulations Governing Non-Military and Non-Naval Transportation On Army and Navy Air Transports

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A further diff-aware amendment extending the already twice-amended EO 9492 (prior batch; extended to 18 months by EO 9629, this batch) fixed sunset from 'eighteen months' to 'two years'; the genuine, disclosed FIXED-DATE sunset structure is preserved, merely lengthened for a third time.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed substitution, plus a new Sec. 3 requiring actual-cost billing (rather than a flat rate) for special-mission flights serving other federal agencies — a disclosed cost-transparency improvement.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The fixed-date sunset, though progressively lengthened across three amendments, remains a genuine date-certain limitation rather than an open-ended grant.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9715—The Death of Harlan Fiske Stone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A purely ceremonial mourning proclamation for the death of Chief Justice Harlan Fiske Stone (flag at half-staff, military honors); no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, standard mourning-protocol directives.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9716—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Railway Express Agency, Inc., and Certain of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9717—Directing the Emergency Board Created By Executive Order 9716 of April 24, 1946 to Investigate a Dispute Between the Railway Express Agency, Inc., and Certain of Its Employees, Also to Investigate Disputes Between the Agency and Certain Other of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow expansion of an already-independent Emergency Board's investigative mandate (adding two more unions' disputes with the same carrier), not a new grant of unilateral power; the board's independence and fixed-deadline structure from EO 9716 remain unchanged.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed extension of the existing independent-board structure.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check.

title: Executive Order 9718—Termination of the Petroleum Administration for War

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A routine wind-down order terminating the Petroleum Administration for War, with liquidation supervision assigned to the Secretary of the Interior, an already-accountable Cabinet official; narrows rather than expands authority, with a fixed effective date (May 8, 1946).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope and fixed effective date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9719—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate Disputes Between the Transcontinental & Western Air, Inc., and Other Carriers, and Certain of Their Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this batch, applied here to the commercial airline industry (TWA, American, United, Eastern, Northwest, Delta, Braniff, and others) rather than railroads.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed independence requirement and fixed statutory deadline.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: One of the earliest applications in this stream of the Railway Labor Act's emergency-board mechanism (extended to air carriers by the 1936 amendment) to a nationwide commercial-airline labor dispute spanning essentially the entire major-carrier industry — reflecting the rapid postwar growth and labor-relations maturation of the airline industry.

title: Executive Order 9720—Discontinuing the Sitka and Subic Bay Naval Airspace Reservations and the Subic Bay, Manila Bay, Los Angeles-Long Beach Harbor, San Diego, San Francisco, Columbia River Entrance, and Strait of Juan De Fuca and Puget Sound Defensive Sea Areas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A mass discontinuation of nine Pacific-theater wartime naval-airspace and defensive-sea-area designations in a single order, matching the established pattern (EO 9648, EO 9650, this batch); narrows rather than expands authority, part of the broader postwar wind-down of coastal/airspace military-security zones.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, each of the nine discontinued designations individually cited to its establishing order.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; this order removes restrictions rather than removing oversight of them.

title: Executive Order 9721—Providing for the Transfer of Personnel to Public International Organizations in Which the United States Government Participates

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, benefits-conferring personnel-transfer mechanism to designated public international organizations, bounded by objective eligibility criteria and a disclosed 3-year sunset on the classified-status-acquisition provision (Sec. 2).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, multi-part objective eligibility test, with CSC authority to prescribe implementing rules.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: Sec. 2 contains a genuine, disclosed fixed-term sunset ('inoperative three years from the date of this order').

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9722—Reassignment of the Functions of the Service of Supply Command and the Commanding General, Services of Supply

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Secretary of War is granted broad discretion to 'reassign to such agencies and officers of the War Department as he may deem appropriate the functions, duties, and powers' of a major wartime Army command structure.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Bounded to a fixed 30-day implementation window, and confined to purely internal War Department reorganization rather than a grant of new external authority.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As he may deem appropriate' is an open-ended operative standard.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9723—Termination of the President's War Relief Control Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A routine wind-down order terminating a wartime relief-oversight board, with liquidation supervision assigned to the Secretary of State, an already-accountable Cabinet official; narrows rather than expands authority, with a fixed effective date.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope and fixed effective date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9724—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9672 Establishing the National Wage Stabilization Board

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical amendment fixing Board-member compensation rates; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed rates.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9725—Designating the Alien Property Custodian to Administer the Powers and Authority Conferred Upon the President by Sections 20 and 32 of the Trading With the Enemy Act, as Amended

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow statutory delegation to an already-established office (Alien Property Custodian), expressly preserving rather than expanding prior delegated authority ('shall not be construed as revoking or limiting any power or authority heretofore delegated').

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed delegation with an explicit non-expansion clause.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9726—Transfer of Fiscal Functions Relating to Lend-lease Matters from the Department of State to the Treasury Department

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow reorganizational transfer of lend-lease fiscal/accounting functions from State to Treasury, both already-accountable Cabinet departments; State retains all non-fiscal lend-lease administration.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope, with a genuine, disclosed joint-recommendation requirement (Sec. 5): the Secretaries of State and Treasury must jointly recommend any rescission of lend-lease appropriations to the Bureau of the Budget.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9727—Possession, Control, and Operation of Certain Railroads

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of the Office of Defense Transportation is granted not merely possession of the enormous named railroad list (over 330 carriers, effectively the entire U.S. rail system) but an explicit, self-expanding 'standing' authority: 'If and when the Director finds it necessary or appropriate to carry out the purposes of this order, he may, by appropriate order, take possession and assume control of all or any part of any transportation system of any OTHER carrier by railroad located in the continental United States' — an unbounded class extending far beyond the named list, matching the 'standing' seizure-authorization architecture first identified in EO 9536 (prior batch) but applied here at unprecedented national scale.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Existing managerial functions, contracts, and collective-bargaining rights are nominally preserved 'except so far as the Director shall from time to time otherwise provide' — a self-graded, non-binding continuity requirement, not an independent check on the Director's own authority.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles possession/operation authority, sub-delegation power, rulemaking authority, and the unbounded standing-seizure clause across effectively the entire national railroad system in a single order — the largest single transportation-infrastructure bundling encountered in this stream, exceeding even EO 9702's extensive carrier list.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such manner as he deems necessary' and similar open standards recur throughout.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 12's termination trigger is entirely self-judged by the Director with no fixed date and no outer cap.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed sunset; termination is wholly contingent on the Director's own, unreviewed determination.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 8 bars, absent the Director's prior written consent, any receivership, reorganization proceeding, or 'attachment by mesne process, garnishment, execution, or otherwise' against seized carrier property — the same judicial/creditor-remedy preemption clause recurring throughout this stream's ODT and War Labor Disputes Act seizure orders (EO 9412, EO 9462, EO 9554, EO 9602, EO 9658, EO 9693, this batch), now applied nationwide; a narrow carve-out preserves already-pending bankruptcy/equity-receivership court proceedings, a partial but not complete limitation on the bar's reach.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: This is the formal seizure order for the nationwide 1946 railroad strike crisis — one of the most consequential domestic labor confrontations of the immediate postwar period, which within days led President Truman to request from Congress standby authority to draft striking railroad workers into the armed forces. Among the highest-scoring orders identified in this entire stream, reflecting both its exceptional structural breadth (unbounded standing authority, nationwide scope, judicial-remedy bar) and its outsized historical significance.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 10: Secretary of War protection-and-assistance-on-request cross-agency cooperation clause, matching the standard ODT seizure template.

title: Executive Order 9729—Further Defining the Functions and Duties of the Office of Defense Transportation

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants the ODT Director broad transportation-equipment allocation authority across rail, motor, water, and air carriers 'upon such conditions, and to such extent, as the said Director shall deem necessary or appropriate in the public interest,' further empowered with investigative and subpoena-like authority (obtaining information, requiring reports, inspecting books and records, administering oaths, compelling witness testimony).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No independent board or external check is specified for either the allocation or investigative authority; accountability runs only through the ordinary chain of command — a nominal, non-binding check.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles broad cross-modal allocation authority together with investigative/subpoena-like powers in a single official in one order.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'As the said Director shall deem necessary or appropriate' is an open-ended operative standard.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No judicial-remedy-bar or similar oversight-preemption clause is present.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 4 expressly preserves, rather than displaces, the Civilian Production Administration's existing authority under EO 9125/EO 9638.

title: Executive Order 9730—Amendment of Executive Order No. 9630 of September 27, 1945, Redistributing Foreign Economic Functions and Functions With Respect to Surplus Property in Foreign Areas

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, single-sentence clarifying amendment to the already-scored EO 9630 (prior batch) that expressly PRESERVES, rather than expands, the Secretary of State's existing personnel and expenditure authority; a disclosed protective clarification, not a new grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9731—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Hudson & Manhattan Railroad Company and Certain of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The same balanced Railway Labor Act §10 emergency-board template used consistently across this batch.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9732—Relating to the Administration of the Act of February 22, 1935, as Amended

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A purely administrative renaming of a Petroleum Administration subdivision ('Petroleum Conservation Division' to 'Oil and Gas Division'); no substantive power change.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9733—Authorizing Certification for Probational Appointment of Certain Classes of Persons

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, benefits-conferring civil-service certification mechanism for veterans who lost a probational-appointment opportunity due to military service, bounded by objective, multi-part eligibility criteria; supersedes and updates the narrower EO 9538 (prior batch).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective multi-part eligibility test.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9734—President's Certificate of Merit

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A purely symbolic civilian-honor establishment (a lesser tier below the Medal for Merit, EO 9637, this batch), administered by the same existing multi-member Board; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed multi-member Board (Medal for Merit Board) administration.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9735—Establishing a Cabinet Committee On Palestine and Related Problems

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Grants the Committee (and its Board of Alternates) broad negotiation authority to 'negotiate and consult, directly or through its representatives, with accredited representatives and agencies of other governments' and other bodies, plus authority to hire technical personnel and consultants 'without regard to the civil service laws and regulations' (Sec. 2(d)).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed multi-Secretary Cabinet Committee structure (State, War, Treasury) with a formally constituted Board of Alternates — a real, multi-member check, not a single official's unilateral authority.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles international negotiation authority, non-civil-service personnel hiring, inter-agency document-procurement authority, and policy-implementation coordination into a single committee in one order.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 2(b)'s written-request requirement for procuring inter-agency records is itself a disclosed, formal accountability channel, not a removal of oversight.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Establishes the Cabinet Committee that translated the Anglo-American Committee of Inquiry's findings (implemented via EO 9682, this batch) into concrete U.S. policy on Palestine and Jewish displaced persons — a pivotal institutional link in the diplomatic chain leading to the 1947 UN Partition Plan and the 1948 founding of Israel, among the most consequential foreign-policy developments of the entire postwar period, despite this order's genuinely multi-member, disclosed committee structure.

title: Executive Order 9736—Possession, Control, and Operation of the Transportation System, Plants, and Facilities of the Monongahela Connecting Railroad Company

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The Director of the Office of Defense Transportation is granted open-ended possession/operation authority over a single named carrier.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: No external board; the Director alone certifies the triggering threat and, per Sec. 5, alone determines termination — a nominal, self-graded check.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: A single named carrier; standard single-entity scope.

flags.name: Vague Enforcement
flags.justification: 'In such manner as he deems necessary' is open-ended.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 5's termination trigger is entirely self-judged with no fixed date and no outer cap.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed sunset.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: Sec. 4 bars, absent the Director's prior written consent, any 'attachment by mesne process, garnishment, execution, or otherwise' — the same recurring judicial/creditor-remedy preemption clause appearing throughout this stream's ODT seizure orders.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: A routine single-carrier seizure matching the standard template; no additional distinct historical significance beyond the pattern already established for this order family.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 7: Secretary of War protection-and-assistance-on-request cross-agency cooperation clause, matching the standard ODT seizure template.

title: Executive Order 9737—Revoking Section 3 of Executive Order 3928 of April 8, 1942, Relating to the Stabilization of Wages, Prices, and Salaries

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow revocation of a defunct wartime agency's (War Manpower Commission) stabilization-related authority; narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9738—Restricting Competition in Examination for the Position of Correctional Officer Under the Bureau of Prisons, Department of Justice

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, veterans'-preference civil-service restriction for a single position class, matching the established EO 9662 (prior batch) template, which this order supersedes and renews.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, single position class and defined statutory duration, with a genuine disclosed exception process requiring Bureau of Prisons Director recommendation for outstanding service.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9740—Amendment of Executive Order No. 1888 of February 2, 1914, as Amended, Relating to Conditions of Employment in the Service of the Panama Canal and the Panama Railroad Company on the Isthmus of Panama

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extraordinarily detailed but purely administrative overhaul of Canal Zone employee leave regulations (accrual, accumulation, commutation, travel leave); the Governor's 'continuing authority to make such additional regulations... as he shall deem necessary' (Sec. 36) is confined to implementing this specific, already-detailed leave system, not an open policy grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, extensively itemized, objective formulas and procedures throughout.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9739—Prescribing Regulations Governing the Payment of Expenses of Transportation of Civilian Officers and Employees Transferred Incident to the Return of Departmental Functions to the Seat of Government

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-implementing (Second Deficiency Appropriation Act, 1946) transportation-expense regulation for agencies returning from wartime dispersal to Washington; objective weight-limit criteria.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective criteria.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9741—Extending the Existence of the Quantico-Superior Committee, Created By Executive Order No. 6783 of June 30, 1934

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, fixed-term (4-year) extension of an existing advisory committee's existence; no new discretionary grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed extension period.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: A genuine, fixed-date extension (to June 30, 1950), not an open-ended continuation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9742—Termination and Liquidation of the War Relocation Authority

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A routine wind-down order terminating the War Relocation Authority as an organizational entity, with liquidation supervision assigned to the Secretary of the Interior, an already-accountable Cabinet official; narrows rather than expands authority, with a fixed effective date.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope and fixed effective date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Terminates the War Relocation Authority, the civilian agency that administered the mass wartime internment of Japanese Americans and Japanese nationals under EO 9102 (March 1942, predating this stream's coding window) — one of the most consequential and, in later historical and legal judgment, unjust exercises of executive power in American history. This termination order itself is a clean, narrow wind-down with no discretionary expansion, but its historical significance as the formal close of the WRA's operations warrants explicit note, distinct in severity from the Hawaii military-authority bookend (EO 9645, this batch) given the WRA's direct administration of forced relocation and detention of over 120,000 people, the substantial majority U.S. citizens, based on ancestry.

title: Executive Order 9743—Regulations Governing the Payment of Expenses of Transportation of the Immediate Families of Civilian Officers and Employees of the Government, Fiscal Year, 1947

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-implementing (Independent Offices Appropriation Act, 1947) transportation-expense regulation for permanently transferred federal employees' families; extends an existing, already-scored regulatory framework (EO 9587, prior batch) to the new fiscal year.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9744—Terminating the Office of the Director of Liquidation and the Liquidation Advisory Committee

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: NOTE: a title/numbering discrepancy is present — the database title reads 'Executive Order 9744-B—Regulations Governing the Furnishing of Clothing in Kind...' (matching an unrelated Navy/Coast Guard clothing-allowance order family), but the corpus text at this number is instead a narrow order terminating the Director of Liquidation and Liquidation Advisory Committee (established by EO 9674, prior batch, in the surplus-property disposal chain), with disclosed BOB-supervised records disposition and a fixed effective date. Coded from the actual corpus text, a routine wind-down order; narrows rather than expands authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed scope and fixed effective date.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9745—Providing for the Interim Administration of Certain Continuing Functions of the Office of Price Administration

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Explicitly disclaims any NEW price/rent-control authority: 'This Executive order shall not be deemed to authorize the Office of Price Administration or the Price Administrator to establish or maintain any regulation or order with respect to current prices or rents, unless and until otherwise provided by legislation hereafter enacted' — a genuine, disclosed self-limitation continuing only already-existing residual (rationing, enforcement-of-past-violations) functions.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, bounded to functions that do not terminate by operation of the expiring statutes.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; if anything, this order narrows OPA's operative authority pending new legislation.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Marks a pivotal moment in OPA's history: the Emergency Price Control Act's expiration on June 30, 1946 (amid an intense congressional battle over renewal) briefly lapsed price-control authority before Congress re-authorized OPA roughly three weeks later via the Price Control Extension Act of 1946 — this interim order bridges that gap by continuing only OPA's non-price residual functions, a notable episode in the broader collapse and near-death of wartime price controls in mid-1946.

title: Executive Order 9746—Authorizing the Secretary of War and the Governor of the Panama Canal to Perform Certain Functions Relating to the Panama Canal and the Canal Zone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Each delegated power is tied to a specific, itemized statutory provision of the Canal Zone Code (canal operation, aircraft/air-navigation, health/quarantine, taxation, alcohol regulation, personnel, entry/detention, military reservations, roads, public-defender compensation), not an open-ended grant; Sec. 5 expressly preserves the President's own right to exercise any of these powers directly.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed multi-level check: the Governor's delegated powers (Sec. 4) require both the 'supervision of the Secretary of War' and, for certain matters, consultation with the ranking U.S. diplomatic officer to Panama.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles a dozen distinct, itemized statutory delegations spanning canal operation, aviation, health, taxation, and law enforcement into a single consolidating order.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the President's own authority is expressly preserved (Sec. 5).

title: Executive Order 9747—Continuing the Functions of the Alien Property Custodian and the Department of the Treasurer in the Philippines

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Continues, rather than expands, existing delegated wartime enemy-property-control authority in the Philippines, pursuant to a specific new congressional authorization (Public Law 485).

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed continuation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Signed the same week as Philippine independence (July 4, 1946) — this order continues U.S. Alien Property Custodian and Treasury enemy-property-control authority within the newly independent Philippines under specific new congressional authorization, a notable marker of the complex transitional legal arrangements accompanying decolonization.

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective category definitions, with CSC approval required for maximum stipend rates.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; CSC approval is a disclosed check.

title: Executive Order 9749—Creating An Emergency Board to Investigate a Dispute Between the Denver & Rio Grande Western Railroad Company and Certain of Its Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9751—Designating Public International Organizations Entitled to Enjoy Certain Privileges, Exemptions, and Immunities

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-implementing designation (International Organizations Immunities Act) adding several additional international organizations to the list begun by EO 9698 (this batch), with a disclosed savings clause preserving the Bretton Woods Agreements Act's own specific immunity provisions for the IBRD.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed statutory basis and savings clause.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Notable as the formal domestic-law immunities designation for the newly created International Monetary Fund and International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (the World Bank), both founded at the 1944 Bretton Woods Conference — foundational institutions of the postwar international economic order, though this order remains a narrow, procedurally clean statutory designation.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

title: Executive Order 9758—Authorizing the Secretary of the Interior to Take Possession of and to Operate Certain Coal Mines

flags.justification: 'As he may deem necessary' is an open-ended operative standard.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: Sec. 8 provides a genuine, disclosed 60-day outer cap on possession after the Secretary determines pre-interruption productive efficiency has been restored — not an open-ended, self-perpetuating trigger.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: The 60-day cap functions as a real, if determination-triggered, sunset.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No judicial-remedy-bar or similar oversight-preemption clause is present in this order.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Notable as a rare instance in this stream of an order explicitly patching a legal gap in an EARLIER seizure order (EO 9728) after a subordinate Deputy Coal Mines Administrator's contrary directive called its effectiveness into question — an unusual, self-correcting administrative-law wrinkle within the recurring coal-seizure pattern (EO 9536, EO 9548, prior batch).

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 4's broad 'All federal agencies are directed to cooperate... to the fullest extent possible' cross-agency cooperation clause, plus a Secretary of War protection-on-request clause (Sec. 5).

title: Executive Order 9759—Suspending Certain Statutory Provisions Relating to Employment in the Canal Zone

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The suspension power is bounded to specific, named statutory sections of three FY1947 appropriations acts (War Department, Navy, Military), rather than an open-ended grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: A congressionally pre-authorized suspension mechanism built into the cited appropriations acts, not unbounded unilateral discretion.

flags.name: Perverse Incentives
flags.justification: The suspension continues 'during the continuance of any of the wars in which the United States is now engaged or of the present national emergency' — an undefined, self-perpetuating trigger with no fixed end date, matching the recurring zombie-emergency pattern found elsewhere in this stream's Canal Zone employment orders.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: No fixed sunset; duration is tied to the undefined ongoing wars/emergency.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No judicial-remedy-bar or similar oversight-preemption clause is present.

title: Executive Order 9760—Conferring Certain Authority Upon the Secretary of State With Regard to Diplomatic and Consular Property of Germany and Japan Within the United States

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-anchored (Trading with the Enemy Act) transfer of control over enemy diplomatic/consular property from the Alien Property Custodian/Treasury to the Secretary of State, with disclosed written-notification requirements governing the handoff between officials.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed inter-official notification requirements (Secs. 2-3) govern any transfer of authority between the Secretary of State, Alien Property Custodian, and Secretary of the Treasury.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9762—Transferring the Functions of the Office of Economic Stablization to the Office of War Mobilization and Reconversion

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Re-concentrates stabilization authority in the single OWMR Director, reversing EO 9699's (this batch) restoration of a broader multi-stakeholder Economic Stabilization Board just five months earlier — a genuine re-concentration of power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The multi-stakeholder Economic Stabilization Board (EO 9699, this batch) is retained only in an advisory capacity — 'for the purpose of advising and consulting with the Director of War Mobilization and Reconversion' — a demotion from its prior decision-making role to a nominal, non-binding advisory one.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No judicial-remedy-bar or similar clause is present.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Marks the THIRD major institutional reconfiguration of the stabilization apparatus within this batch alone (EO 9620's single-Administrator model, prior batch, superseded by EO 9699's restored multi-stakeholder Board, this batch, now re-concentrated under OWMR by this order), vividly illustrating the stabilization program's severe institutional instability in mid-1946 as it approached the expiration of, and contentious congressional battle over, price-control renewal — a historically significant marker of the wartime stabilization program's terminal decline.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Facilitates continued tax-return access for the Special Committee to Investigate the National Defense Program (the 'Truman Committee'), the Senate investigative body whose rigorous 1941-1944 oversight of wartime contracting waste and fraud had made then-Senator Truman's national reputation and was a significant factor in his selection as FDR's 1944 running mate — a notable point of historical continuity as President Truman here facilitates the continued work of his own former investigative committee.

title: Executive Order 9784—Providing for the More Efficient Use and for the Transfer and Other Disposition of Government Records

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: The requirement that Director approval govern any inter-agency records transfer is itself a disclosed check on individual agencies' own information practices, and Sec. 5 provides a genuine, disclosed confidentiality carve-out for records whose disclosure 'would endanger the national interest or the lives of individuals.'

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles government-wide records-retention policy, inter-agency-transfer approval authority, personnel-records regulation (delegated to CSC), and National Archives coordination into a single comprehensive management order.

flags.justification: 'As he may deem necessary' recurs as an open standard for the Director's inspection and directive authority (Sec. 4).

flags.name: Power Concentration

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Terminates the standalone Office of Alien Property Custodian (EO 9095, outside this stream's coding window, substantially rewritten by EO 9193, which scored CRITICAL 70% in a prior batch for its explicit anti-judicial-review clause) and folds its enemy-property administration into the Department of Justice — a historically significant institutional consolidation that created the lasting DOJ Office of Alien Property function, moving this powerful wartime seizure authority from a standalone Executive Office position into an ordinary, judicially-reviewable Cabinet department context. Note: this order was apparently issued in duplicate — EO 9788 and EO 9789 (this batch) share identical substantive text and the same date; both are coded identically, with the duplication disclosed rather than treated as two distinct policy actions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order creates, rather than removes, an investigative check on both parties.

title: Executive Order 9804—Revoking Executive Orders No. 2463 of September 29, 1916, and No. 4182 of March 24, 1925, Relating to Administration of the Employees' Compensation Act With Respect to Employees of Alaska Railroad, and Placing the Administration of That Act as to Such Employees in the Federal Security Agency

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow reorganizational transfer of a compensation-administration function to the Federal Security Agency, consistent with a broader 1946 Reorganization Plan; no new discretionary grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed effective date (January 1, 1947).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9806—Establishing the President's Temporary Commission on Employee Loyalty

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A study/investigative commission only, with no independent enforcement or adjudicative power — it can 'inquire into' existing standards and 'make... recommendations,' not remove or disqualify any employee itself.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed six-agency interagency board structure (Justice, State, Treasury, War, Navy, CSC), Justice-chaired, no single unchecked official.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed reporting deadline (February 1, 1947) with automatic termination of the Commission upon submitting its report, 'unless otherwise authorized by further Executive order' — a real, task-completion-triggered sunset rather than an open-ended continuation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Establishes the direct institutional precursor to Truman's landmark Federal Employee Loyalty Program (EO 9835, March 1947, just outside this batch's coding window) — the postwar loyalty-security apparatus that, in the following decade, became a central mechanism of Cold War-era anti-communist enforcement and, in its later, expanded forms, a significant instrument of the McCarthyist abuses that damaged the careers and reputations of thousands of federal employees based on often flimsy or unsubstantiated allegations. This order itself is a narrow, disclosed, multi-agency STUDY commission with no independent adjudicative or enforcement power — it could only investigate and recommend — but its historical significance as the origin point of that later apparatus is exceptional and warrants explicit, careful flagging even though the text itself reads as structurally modest.

title: Executive Order 9805—Regulations Governing Payment of Travel and Transportation Expenses of Civilian Officers and Employees of the United States When Transferred from One Official Station to Another for Permanent Duty

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An extraordinarily detailed but purely administrative transportation-expense regulation (statute-implementing, Public Law 600), consolidating and superseding five prior orders; objective weight limits, rate schedules, and time limits govern throughout, not discretionary grants.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, extensively itemized, objective formulas and procedures.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9807—Authorizing the Civil Service Commission to Confer a Competitive Civil Service Status Upon Certain Groups of Employees

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A protective, benefits-conferring civil-service status grant for employees of several defunct New Deal-era work-relief programs, bounded by objective, fixed continuous-service dates.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective eligibility criteria administered by the Civil Service Commission itself.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9809—Providing for the Disposition of Certain War Agencies

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Consolidates FOUR major wartime economic-control agencies — the Office of War Mobilization and Reconversion, the Office of Economic Stabilization, the Office of Price Administration, and the Civilian Production Administration — into a single new Office of Temporary Controls under one Administrator, who is vested with 'the functions of the Director of War Mobilization and Reconversion, the Economic Stabilization Director, the Price Administrator, and the Civilian Production Administrator' simultaneously — the most extensive single-official concentration of wartime economic authority encountered in this batch, exceeding even EO 9599's apex-coordinator design.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Sec. 3(a) preserves an advisory board (from the War Mobilization and Reconversion Act) in only an advisory capacity; Sec. 3(b) explicitly TERMINATES the Economic Stabilization Board outright — removing the last vestige of the multi-stakeholder check structure restored by EO 9699 (this batch) and already demoted to advisory status by EO 9762 (this batch), leaving the new Administrator with essentially no independent board oversight at all — a nominal, non-binding check at best.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles the entire remaining wartime price-control, wage-stabilization, production-control, and reconversion-coordination apparatus into a single agency in one order, additionally redistributing numerous ancillary functions (contract settlement to Treasury, financial reporting to the FTC, wage-board functions to Labor/Treasury, re-establishment of the Office of Government Reports) — an extraordinarily complex, multi-directional consolidation.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No judicial-remedy-bar or similar oversight-preemption clause is present.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Marks the FOURTH and final major institutional reconfiguration of the wartime stabilization apparatus within about fifteen months (EO 9620, prior batch, single-Administrator model → EO 9699, this batch, restored multi-stakeholder Board → EO 9762, this batch, re-concentrated under OWMR → this order), consolidating what remained of OPA, OWMR, OES, and CPA into a single 'Office of Temporary Controls' as the wartime economic-control system entered its terminal wind-down phase — a historically significant capstone to that sequence, vividly illustrating the endgame of wartime economic controls roughly six months before OPA's final abolition in 1947.

flags.name: Inter-Agency Cannibalization
flags.justification: Sec. 10(c) creates 'special board or boards... constituted as may be necessary by the Secretary of Labor' to inherit some National Wage Stabilization Board functions — a notable cross-agency functional redistribution, alongside similar splits of other functions to Treasury and the FTC.

title: Executive Order 9810—Excusing Federal Employees from Duty One-half Day On December 24, 1946

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A purely administrative, government-wide holiday-schedule order; no discretionary power grant.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, with defined exceptions for special public need.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9811—Extension of Trust Periods On Indian Lands Expiring During the Calendar Year 1947

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: The 1947 iteration of the same protective, beneficiary-favoring blanket trust-period extension (25 years) already scored for EO 9500 and EO 9659 (prior and this batch), with the same disclosed congressional-reservation carve-out preserved.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, fixed 25-year extension term with the same disclosed congressional-reservation carve-out.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: The order preserves rather than displaces Congress's own reserved authority.

title: Executive Order 9812—Inspection of Income, Excess-profits, and Declared Value Excess-profits Tax Returns By the Committee On Naval Affairs, House of Representatives

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: An oversight-enhancing tax-return inspection authorization for a House committee, consistent with the recurring pattern of such orders in this stream.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, subject to Treasury-decision conditions.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: This order enables oversight rather than removing it.

title: Executive Order 9813—Appointment of the Members and the Alternate Member of a Military Tribunal Established for the Trial and Punishment of Major War Criminals in Germany

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow personnel designation of judges to a specific U.S. military tribunal under Control Council Law No. 10; not an independent grant of discretionary domestic power.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, multiple named individuals with a clear quadripartite international legal basis.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Designates judges for the U.S. Military Tribunals established under Control Council Law No. 10 — the 'Subsequent Nuremberg Trials' (including the historically pivotal Doctors' Trial, which produced the Nuremberg Code governing medical ethics and human-subjects research, still foundational to bioethics today) — a continuation of the historically monumental Nuremberg prosecution apparatus first flagged at EO 9547/EO 9626 (prior and this batch), warranting the same CRITICAL Flag 9 historical-significance treatment despite this order's narrow, cleanly disclosed personnel-designation text.

title: Executive Order 9814—Establishing An Amnesty Board to Review Convictions of Persons Under the Selective Training and Service Act of 1940 and to Make Recommendations for Executive Clemency With Respect Thereto

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A review/recommendation board only; final clemency authority remains with the President's own Article II pardon power, and the Board has no independent enforcement or adjudicative authority.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed three-member Board with a clear reporting chain through the Attorney General to the President.

flags.name: Sunset Provisions (absence of)
flags.justification: A genuine, disclosed task-completion sunset: the Board 'shall cease to exist, unless otherwise provided by further Executive order, upon the submission of its final recommendations.'

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: Establishes a clemency-review mechanism for the thousands of Americans convicted of Selective Service Act violations during WWII (including conscientious objectors and other draft resisters) — a historically notable, humane use of executive clemency power to reconsider wartime prosecutions in the immediate postwar period, though the Board itself has only recommendatory, not adjudicative or self-executing, authority.

title: Executive Order 9815—Amending Section 7 of the Executive Order No. 9691 of February 4, 1946, Entitled "Directing the Civil Service Commission to Resume Operations Under the Civil Service Rules, and Authorizing the Adoption of Special Regulations During the Transitional Period"

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, technical amendment that further narrows (rather than expands) an existing transitional civil-service employment provision by adding a Schedule A-tied termination condition; restricts rather than broadens discretion.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, objective condition.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.

title: Executive Order 9816—Providing the Transfer of Properties and Personnel to the Atomic Energy Commission

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: Implements a specific congressional statute — the Atomic Energy Act of 1946 (the McMahon Act) — establishing CIVILIAN control over the Manhattan Project's military-run infrastructure; a genuine de-concentration from War Department/military control to a civilian Commission, in accordance with the Act's central civilian-control design principle.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Genuine, disclosed multi-step, three-tier dispute-resolution mechanism for excepted property (Sec. 2): Secretary of War notification, Commission review, and unresolved disputes referred to the President for final decision — a real, disclosed layered check, not unilateral discretion by either party.

flags.name: Bundling
flags.justification: Bundles an extraordinarily comprehensive transfer — fissionable material, weapons, facilities, contracts, patents, personnel, and records — across the entire nuclear-weapons complex in a single order.

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed; the Sec. 2 dispute-resolution mechanism is itself a new, disclosed check.

flags.name: Second/Third-Order Effects
flags.justification: This is the founding transfer order for the Atomic Energy Commission, implementing the Atomic Energy Act of 1946 and completing the historically pivotal transition of the entire U.S. nuclear-weapons and atomic-energy infrastructure from exclusive War Department/military control (the Manhattan Engineer District) to CIVILIAN Commission control — among the most consequential institutional events in the history of postwar U.S. nuclear governance, establishing the civilian-control principle that has shaped American nuclear policy, arms control, and non-proliferation architecture ever since. Structurally the order itself is a clean, disclosed property-transfer mechanism with genuine dispute-resolution safeguards, illustrating once again this rubric's central finding that historical magnitude and structural-weight score can diverge sharply.

title: Executive Order 9817—Regulations Governing Awards to Federal Employees for Meritorious Suggestions and for Exceptional or Meritorious Service

flags.name: Power Concentration
flags.justification: A narrow, statute-implementing (Public Law 600, the Government Employees' Incentive Awards Act) employee-suggestion award program, bounded by a disclosed $25,000 annual cap per department and objective award criteria.

flags.name: Accountability Gaps
flags.justification: Fully disclosed, with mandatory annual reporting to the Bureau of the Budget (Sec. 4).

flags.name: Preemption of Oversight
flags.justification: No oversight is removed.
